{{混同|Yamakawa Onsen}} {{Japanese Hot Spring | Name = Yamaga Onsen (山鹿温泉) | image = [[File:Sakura-yu Yamaga ac (1).jpg|280px]]
Communal bath {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯}} | Comment = | Location = {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}{{ill|Yamaga City|ja|山鹿市}} {{Japanese Location Information|33|0|53.1|130|41|20.3|Yamaga Onsen|33.01475,130.688972|Yamaga Onsen|nocoord=yes}} {{Location map|Japan Kumamoto Prefecture|relief=1|float=center|width=225}} | Latitude Degrees = 33 | Latitude Minutes = 0 | Latitude Seconds = 53.1 | Longitude Degrees = 130 | Longitude Minutes = 41 | Longitude Seconds = 20.3 | Access = ''For details, see the [[#Access|Access]] section.'' | Spring Quality = {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉}} | Spring Temperature = | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = Alkaline | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = [https://yamaga-tanbou.jp/genre/onsen/ Official site of Yamaga Onsen in Yamaga City] | Special Notes = }} '''Yamaga Onsen''' (やまがおんせん) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉}} located in {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}{{ill|Yamaga City|ja|山鹿市}} (formerly part of {{ill|Higo Province|ja|肥後国}}). It is renowned for the annual "Yamaga Lantern Festival" ({{ill|Yamaga Tōrō Matsuri|ja|山鹿灯籠まつり}}) held every August. A communal bath (public bath) known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯}} is available. == Spring Quality == * Alkaline simple hot spring It is reputed for its exceptionally smooth water that is said to have excellent beautifying effects. == Hot Spring Town == [[File:yamaga_ashiyu.jpg|thumb|"Ashiyu"]] There are approximately 20 establishments including {{ill|ryokan|ja|旅館}} and {{ill|hotels|ja|ホテル}}, making it one of the largest hot spring towns in Kumamoto Prefecture. As an old hot spring town, it emphasizes its historic charm, and it is said that during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}} it was modeled after {{ill|Dogo Onsen|ja|道後温泉}}. Although it is located within the urban area, creatively designed open-air baths and bathing rooms enhance its quaint atmosphere. In addition to "Ashiyu" and communal baths, a hot spring pool (now no longer extant) was once established. Along the former {{ill|Buzen Highway|ja|豊前街道}}, there stands the Yachiyo-za, a theater building completed in 1910 (Meiji 45). === Communal Bath === A communal bath known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯}} exists. It was originally constructed by inviting the master carpenter who built the main building of {{ill|Dogo Onsen Honkan|ja|道後温泉本館}}. In a redevelopment in 1973 (Shōwa 48), it was incorporated into the Plaza Five Building, preserving only the original eave ({{ill|hafu|ja|破風}}).{{要出典範囲|The interior was modern and at first resembled a pool, yet the water flowing from the lion-shaped spout retained the characteristic smoothness of Yamaga Onsen.|date=2024-08}} In July 2011 (Heisei 23), redevelopment work on the Plaza Five Building commenced, and in October 2012 (Heisei 24) a restoration of traditional Japanese architecture was completed.{{独自研究範囲|Primarily used by local residents.|date=2024-08}}{{要出典範囲|A drinking facility existed in front of the old building’s entrance, providing potable hot spring water 24 hours a day.|date=2024-08}} == History == === Origins === In ancient times, the {{ill|Wamyo Ruijusho|ja|倭名類聚抄}}, a dictionary from the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, records the place name "Yun" (温泉) in "Yamaga-gun" (山鹿郡). Yamaga Onsen is considered one of the oldest hot spring resorts in Kumamoto Prefecture, and several legends regarding its origin persist. One theory posits that during the {{ill|Hōgen Rebellion|ja|保元の乱}}, after being defeated and retreating from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}}, {{ill|Uno Chika-ji|ja|宇野親治}} was hunting in the mountains and discovered the hot spring upon seeing a herd of {{ill|deer|ja|鹿}} lying in a valley. The name "Yamaga" is said to derive from this {{ill|Hot Spring Discovery Legend|ja|開湯伝説}}. Another tradition holds that a descendant of Kikuchi Noritaka’s second son, Masataka—namely, the progenitor of Yamaga Sogyo, {{ill|Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|ja|山鹿太郎重光}}—relocated people to the area in the 3rd year of the {{ill|Jishō period|ja|治承}} and constructed bathing facilities, thus laying the foundation for the growth of Yamaga town. === Early Modern Period === During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, as a post town, Yamaga Onsen flourished to the extent that it was said there was "not a single one of a thousand houses missing." At the site where Uno Chika-ji discovered the deer, a bathhouse for the feudal lord was established, and an imperial bath ({{ill|Gomae-yu|ja|御前湯}}) was installed. === Modern and Contemporary Periods === In 1923 (Taishō 12), the Yamaga Onsen Railway (later known as the {{ill|Yamaga Onsen Railway|ja|山鹿温泉鉄道}}) began operations, with {{ill|Yamaga Station|ja|山鹿駅}} serving as the nearest station; however, it was discontinued in 1960 (Shōwa 35) due to flooding. In November 2006 (Heisei 18), together with three other hot spring areas in the Kikuchi River basin—{{ill|Tama Onsen|ja|玉名温泉}}, {{ill|Kikuchi Onsen|ja|菊池温泉}}, and {{ill|Ueki Onsen|ja|植木温泉}}—the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort Development Council" was established, aiming to brand the area as the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort" and to develop common products and wide-area tourism routes. == Access == * From the {{ill|Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal|ja|熊本桜町バスターミナル}}, take the {{ill|Kyushu Sankō Bus|ja|九州産交バス}} bound for Yamaga Onsen and alight at "Yamaga Onsen (Yachiyo-za Entrance)." * Approximately 12 km from the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}} {{ill|Ueki Interchange|ja|植木インターチェンジ}}, and 9.5 km from the {{ill|Kikusui Interchange|ja|菊水インターチェンジ}}. == Related Topics == * Hot springs in Yamaga City ** {{ill|Hirayama Onsen|ja|平山温泉}} ** {{ill|Kumairi Onsen|ja|熊入温泉}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Yamaga Onsen}} {{Wiki Portal Link|Hot Springs|[[File:Hot springs 001.svg|34px|Portal: Hot Springs]]}} * [https://www.y-kankoukyoukai.com/ {{ill|Yamaga Onsen Tourism Association|ja|山鹿温泉観光協会}}] * [https://yamaga-tanbou.jp/genre/onsen/ {{ill|Yamaga Onsen|ja|山鹿温泉}}] – revision.text:
{{混同|Yamakawa Onsen}} {{Japanese Hot Spring | Name = Yamaga Onsen (山鹿温泉) | image = [[File:Sakura-yu Yamaga ac (1).jpg|280px]]
Communal bath {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯}} | Comment = | Location = {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}{{ill|Yamaga City|ja|山鹿市}} {{Japanese Location Information|33|0|53.1|130|41|20.3|Yamaga Onsen|33.01475,130.688972|Yamaga Onsen|nocoord=yes}} {{Location map|Japan Kumamoto Prefecture|relief=1|float=center|width=225}} | Latitude Degrees = 33 | Latitude Minutes = 0 | Latitude Seconds = 53.1 | Longitude Degrees = 130 | Longitude Minutes = 41 | Longitude Seconds = 20.3 | Access = ''For details, see the [[#Access|Access]] section.'' | Spring Quality = {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉}} | Spring Temperature = | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = Alkaline | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = [https://yamaga-tanbou.jp/genre/onsen/ Official site of Yamaga Onsen in Yamaga City] | Special Notes = }} '''Yamaga Onsen''' (やまがおんせん) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉}} located in {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}{{ill|Yamaga City|ja|山鹿市}} (formerly part of {{ill|Higo Province|ja|肥後国}}). It is renowned for the annual "Yamaga Lantern Festival" ({{ill|Yamaga Tōrō Matsuri|ja|山鹿灯籠まつり}}) held every August. A communal bath (public bath) known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯}} is available. == Spring Quality == * Alkaline simple hot spring It is reputed for its exceptionally smooth water that is said to have excellent beautifying effects. == Hot Spring Town == [[File:yamaga_ashiyu.jpg|thumb|"Ashiyu"]] There are approximately 20 establishments including {{ill|ryokan|ja|旅館}} and {{ill|hotels|ja|ホテル}}, making it one of the largest hot spring towns in Kumamoto Prefecture. As an old hot spring town, it emphasizes its historic charm, and it is said that during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}} it was modeled after {{ill|Dogo Onsen|ja|道後温泉}}. Although it is located within the urban area, creatively designed open-air baths and bathing rooms enhance its quaint atmosphere. In addition to "Ashiyu" and communal baths, a hot spring pool (now no longer extant) was once established. Along the former {{ill|Buzen Highway|ja|豊前街道}}, there stands the Yachiyo-za, a theater building completed in 1910 (Meiji 45). === Communal Bath === A communal bath known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯}} exists. It was originally constructed by inviting the master carpenter who built the main building of {{ill|Dogo Onsen Honkan|ja|道後温泉本館}}. In a redevelopment in 1973 (Shōwa 48), it was incorporated into the Plaza Five Building, preserving only the original eave ({{ill|hafu|ja|破風}}).{{要出典範囲|The interior was modern and at first resembled a pool, yet the water flowing from the lion-shaped spout retained the characteristic smoothness of Yamaga Onsen.|date=2024-08}} In July 2011 (Heisei 23), redevelopment work on the Plaza Five Building commenced, and in October 2012 (Heisei 24) a restoration of traditional Japanese architecture was completed.{{独自研究範囲|Primarily used by local residents.|date=2024-08}}{{要出典範囲|A drinking facility existed in front of the old building’s entrance, providing potable hot spring water 24 hours a day.|date=2024-08}} == History == === Origins === In ancient times, the {{ill|Wamyo Ruijusho|ja|倭名類聚抄}}, a dictionary from the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, records the place name "Yun" (温泉) in "Yamaga-gun" (山鹿郡). Yamaga Onsen is considered one of the oldest hot spring resorts in Kumamoto Prefecture, and several legends regarding its origin persist. One theory posits that during the {{ill|Hōgen Rebellion|ja|保元の乱}}, after being defeated and retreating from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}}, {{ill|Uno Chika-ji|ja|宇野親治}} was hunting in the mountains and discovered the hot spring upon seeing a herd of {{ill|deer|ja|鹿}} lying in a valley. The name "Yamaga" is said to derive from this {{ill|Hot Spring Discovery Legend|ja|開湯伝説}}. Another tradition holds that a descendant of Kikuchi Noritaka’s second son, Masataka—namely, the progenitor of Yamaga Sogyo, {{ill|Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|ja|山鹿太郎重光}}—relocated people to the area in the 3rd year of the {{ill|Jishō period|ja|治承}} and constructed bathing facilities, thus laying the foundation for the growth of Yamaga town. === Early Modern Period === During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, as a post town, Yamaga Onsen flourished to the extent that it was said there was "not a single one of a thousand houses missing." At the site where Uno Chika-ji discovered the deer, a bathhouse for the feudal lord was established, and an imperial bath ({{ill|Gomae-yu|ja|御前湯}}) was installed. === Modern and Contemporary Periods === In 1923 (Taishō 12), the Yamaga Onsen Railway (later known as the {{ill|Yamaga Onsen Railway|ja|山鹿温泉鉄道}}) began operations, with {{ill|Yamaga Station|ja|山鹿駅}} serving as the nearest station; however, it was discontinued in 1960 (Shōwa 35) due to flooding. In November 2006 (Heisei 18), together with three other hot spring areas in the Kikuchi River basin—{{ill|Tama Onsen|ja|玉名温泉}}, {{ill|Kikuchi Onsen|ja|菊池温泉}}, and {{ill|Ueki Onsen|ja|植木温泉}}—the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort Development Council" was established, aiming to brand the area as the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort" and to develop common products and wide-area tourism routes. == Access == * From the {{ill|Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal|ja|熊本桜町バスターミナル}}, take the {{ill|Kyushu Sankō Bus|ja|九州産交バス}} bound for Yamaga Onsen and alight at "Yamaga Onsen (Yachiyo-za Entrance)." * Approximately 12 km from the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}} {{ill|Ueki Interchange|ja|植木インターチェンジ}}, and 9.5 km from the {{ill|Kikusui Interchange|ja|菊水インターチェンジ}}. == Related Topics == * Hot springs in Yamaga City ** {{ill|Hirayama Onsen|ja|平山温泉}} ** {{ill|Kumairi Onsen|ja|熊入温泉}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Yamaga Onsen}} {{Wiki Portal Link|Hot Springs|[[File:Hot springs 001.svg|34px|Portal: Hot Springs]]}} * [https://www.y-kankoukyoukai.com/ {{ill|Yamaga Onsen Tourism Association|ja|山鹿温泉観光協会}}] * [https://yamaga-tanbou.jp/genre/onsen/ {{ill|Yamaga Onsen|ja|山鹿温泉}}] – Yamaga City {{Hot Spring}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やまかおんせん}} [[Category:Hot Springs in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamaga City]] [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E9%B9%BF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] Onsen]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yamaga Onsen]][[Category:qq]] {{Onsen-stub}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamaga Onsen}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yamaga Onsen]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yamaga Onsen]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yamaga Onsen]][[Category:qq]] It was originally constructed by inviting the master carpenter who built the main building of {{ill|Dogo Onsen Honkan|ja|道後温泉本館}}. In a redevelopment in 1973 (Shōwa 48), it was incorporated into the Plaza Five Building, preserving only the original eave ({{ill|hafu|ja|破風}}).{{要出典範囲|The interior was modern and at first resembled a pool, yet the water flowing from the lion-shaped spout retained the characteristic smoothness of Yamaga Onsen.|date=2024-08}} In July 2011 (Heisei 23), redevelopment work on the Plaza Five Building commenced, and in October 2012 (Heisei 24) a restoration of traditional Japanese architecture was completed.{{独自研究範囲|Primarily used by local residents.|date=2024-08}}{{要出典範囲|A drinking facility existed in front of the old building’s entrance, providing potable hot spring water 24 hours a day.|date=2024-08}} == History == === Origins === In ancient times, the {{ill|Wamyo Ruijusho|ja|倭名類聚抄}}, a dictionary from the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, records the place name "Yun" (温泉) in "Yamaga-gun" (山鹿郡). Yamaga Onsen is considered one of the oldest hot spring resorts in Kumamoto Prefecture, and several legends regarding its origin persist. One theory posits that during the {{ill|Hōgen Rebellion|ja|保元の乱}}, after being defeated and retreating from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}}, {{ill|Uno Chika-ji|ja|宇野親治}} was hunting in the mountains and discovered the hot spring upon seeing a herd of {{ill|deer|ja|鹿}} lying in a valley. The name "Yamaga" is said to derive from this {{ill|Hot Spring Discovery Legend|ja|開湯伝説}}. Another tradition holds that a descendant of Kikuchi Noritaka’s second son, Masataka—namely, the progenitor of Yamaga Sogyo, {{ill|Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|ja|山鹿太郎重光}}—relocated people to the area in the 3rd year of the {{ill|Jishō period|ja|治承}} and constructed bathing facilities, thus laying the foundation for the growth of Yamaga town. === Early Modern Period === During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, as a post town, Yamaga Onsen flourished to the extent that it was said there was "not a single one of a thousand houses missing." At the site where Uno Chika-ji discovered the deer, a bathhouse for the feudal lord was established, and an imperial bath ({{ill|Gomae-yu|ja|御前湯}}) was installed. === Modern and Contemporary Periods === In 1923 (Taishō 12), the Yamaga Onsen Railway (later known as the {{ill|Yamaga Onsen Railway|ja|山鹿温泉鉄道}}) began operations, with {{ill|Yamaga Station|ja|山鹿駅}} serving as the nearest station; however, it was discontinued in 1960 (Shōwa 35) due to flooding. In November 2006 (Heisei 18), together with three other hot spring areas in the Kikuchi River basin—{{ill|Tama Onsen|ja|玉名温泉}}, {{ill|Kikuchi Onsen|ja|菊池温泉}}, and {{ill|Ueki Onsen|ja|植木温泉}}—the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort Development Council" was established, aiming to brand the area as the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort" and to develop common products and wide-area tourism routes. == Access == * From the {{ill|Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal|ja|熊本桜町バスターミナル}}, take the {{ill|Kyushu Sankō Bus|ja|九州産交バス}} bound for Yamaga Onsen and alight at "Yamaga Onsen (Yachiyo-za Entrance)." * Approximately 12 km from the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}} {{ill|Ueki Interchange|ja|植木インターチェンジ}}, and 9.5 km from the {{ill|Kikusui Interchange|ja|菊水インターチェンジ}}. == Related Topics == * Hot springs in Yamaga City ** {{ill|Hirayama Onsen|ja|平山温泉}} ** {{ill|Kumairi Onsen|ja|熊入温泉}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Yamaga Onsen}} {{Wiki Portal Link|Hot Springs|[[File:Hot springs {{混同|Yamakawa Onsen}} {{Japanese Hot Spring | Name = Yamaga Onsen (山鹿温泉) | image = [[File:Sakura-yu Yamaga ac (1).jpg|280px]]
Communal bath {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯}} | Comment = | Location = {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}{{ill|Yamaga City|ja|山鹿市}} {{Japanese Location Information|33|0|53.1|130|41|20.3|Yamaga Onsen|33.01475,130.688972|Yamaga Onsen|nocoord=yes}} {{Location map|Japan Kumamoto Prefecture|relief=1|float=center|width=225}} | Latitude Degrees = 33 | Latitude Minutes = 0 | Latitude Seconds = 53.1 | Longitude Degrees = 130 | Longitude Minutes = 41 | Longitude Seconds = 20.3 | Access = ''For details, see the [[#Access|Access]] section.'' | Spring Quality = {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉}} | Spring Temperature = | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = Alkaline | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = [https://yamaga-tanbou.jp/genre/onsen/ Official site of Yamaga Onsen in Yamaga City] | Special Notes = }} '''Yamaga Onsen''' (やまがおんせん) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉}} located in {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}{{ill|Yamaga City|ja|山鹿市}} (formerly part of {{ill|Higo Province|ja|肥後国}}). It is renowned for the annual "Yamaga Lantern Festival" ({{ill|Yamaga Tōrō Matsuri|ja|山鹿灯籠まつり}}) held every August. A communal bath (public bath) known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯}} is available. == Spring Quality == * Alkaline simple hot spring It is reputed for its exceptionally smooth water that is said to have excellent beautifying effects. == Hot Spring Town == [[File:yamaga_ashiyu.jpg|thumb|"Ashiyu"]] There are approximately 20 establishments including {{ill|ryokan|ja|旅館}} and {{ill|hotels|ja|ホテル}}, making it one of the largest hot spring towns in Kumamoto Prefecture. As an old hot spring town, it emphasizes its historic charm, and it is said that during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}} it was modeled after {{ill|Dogo Onsen|ja|道後温泉}}. Although it is located within the urban area, creatively designed open-air baths and bathing rooms enhance its quaint atmosphere. In addition to "Ashiyu" and communal baths, a hot spring pool (now no longer extant) was once established. Along the former {{ill|Buzen Highway|ja|豊前街道}}, there stands the Yachiyo-za, a theater building completed in 1910 (Meiji 45). === Communal Bath === A communal bath known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯}} exists. It was originally constructed by inviting the master carpenter who built the main building of {{ill|Dogo Onsen Honkan|ja|道後温泉本館}}. In a redevelopment in 1973 (Shōwa 48), it was incorporated into the Plaza Five Building, preserving only the original eave ({{ill|hafu|ja|破風}}).{{要出典範囲|The interior was modern and at first resembled a pool, yet the water flowing from the lion-shaped spout retained the characteristic smoothness of Yamaga Onsen.|date=2024-08}} In July 2011 (Heisei 23), redevelopment work on the Plaza Five Building commenced, and in October 2012 (Heisei 24) a restoration of traditional Japanese architecture was completed.{{独自研究範囲|Primarily used by local residents.|date=2024-08}}{{要出典範囲|A drinking facility existed in front of the old building’s entrance, providing potable hot spring water 24 hours a day.|date=2024-08}} == History == === Origins === In ancient times, the {{ill|Wamyo Ruijusho|ja|倭名類聚抄}}, a dictionary from the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, records the place name "Yun" (温泉) in "Yamaga-gun" (山鹿郡). Yamaga Onsen is considered one of the oldest hot spring resorts in Kumamoto Prefecture, and several legends regarding its origin persist. One theory posits that during the {{ill|Hōgen Rebellion|ja|保元の乱}}, after being defeated and retreating from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}}, {{ill|Uno Chika-ji|ja|宇野親治}} was hunting in the mountains and discovered the hot spring upon seeing a herd of {{ill|deer|ja|鹿}} lying in a valley. The name "Yamaga" is said to derive from this {{ill|Hot Spring Discovery Legend|ja|開湯伝説}}. Another tradition holds that a descendant of Kikuchi Noritaka’s second son, Masataka—namely, the progenitor of Yamaga Sogyo, {{ill|Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|ja|山鹿太郎重光}}—relocated people to the area in the 3rd year of the {{ill|Jishō period|ja|治承}} and constructed bathing facilities, thus laying the foundation for the growth of Yamaga town. === Early Modern Period === During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, as a post town, Yamaga Onsen flourished to the extent that it was said there was "not a single one of a thousand houses missing." In November 2006 (Heisei 18), together with three other hot spring areas in the Kikuchi River basin—{{ill|Tama Onsen|ja|玉名温泉}}, {{ill|Kikuchi Onsen|ja|菊池温泉}}, and {{ill|Ueki Onsen|ja|植木温泉}}—the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort Development Council" was established, aiming to brand the area as the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort" and to develop common products and wide-area tourism routes. == Access == {{混同|Yamakawa Onsen}} {{Japanese Hot Spring | Name = Yamaga Onsen (山鹿温泉) | image = [[File:Sakura-yu Yamaga ac (1).jpg|280px]]
Communal bath {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯}} | Comment = | Location = {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}{{ill|Yamaga City|ja|山鹿市}} {{Japanese Location Information|33|0|53.1|130|41|20.3|Yamaga Onsen|33.01475,130.688972|Yamaga Onsen|nocoord=yes}} {{Location map|Japan Kumamoto Prefecture|relief=1|float=center|width=225}} | Latitude Degrees = 33 | Latitude Minutes = 0 | Latitude Seconds = 53.1 | Longitude Degrees = 130 | Longitude Minutes = 41 | Longitude Seconds = 20.3 | Access = ''For details, see the [[#Access|Access]] section.'' | Spring Quality = {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉}} | Spring Temperature = | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = Alkaline | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = [https://yamaga-tanbou.jp/genre/onsen/ Official site of Yamaga Onsen in Yamaga City] | Special Notes = }} '''Yamaga Onsen''' (やまがおんせん) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉}} located in {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}{{ill|Yamaga City|ja|山鹿市}} (formerly part of {{ill|Higo Province|ja|肥後国}}). It is renowned for the annual "Yamaga Lantern Festival" ({{ill|Yamaga Tōrō Matsuri|ja|山鹿灯籠まつり}}) held every August. A communal bath (public bath) known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯}} is available. == Spring Quality == * Alkaline simple hot spring It is reputed for its exceptionally smooth water that is said to have excellent beautifying effects. == Hot Spring Town == [[File:yamaga_ashiyu.jpg|thumb|"Ashiyu"]] There are approximately 20 establishments including {{ill|ryokan|ja|旅館}} and {{ill|hotels|ja|ホテル}}, making it one of the largest hot spring towns in Kumamoto Prefecture. As an old hot spring town, it emphasizes its historic charm, and it is said that during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}} it was modeled after {{ill|Dogo Onsen|ja|道後温泉}}. Although it is located within the urban area, creatively designed open-air baths and bathing rooms enhance its quaint atmosphere. In addition to "Ashiyu" and communal baths, a hot spring pool (now no longer extant) was once established. Along the former {{ill|Buzen Highway|ja|豊前街道}}, there stands the Yachiyo-za, a theater building completed in 1910 (Meiji 45). === Communal Bath === A communal bath known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯}} exists. It was originally constructed by inviting the master carpenter who built the main building of {{ill|Dogo Onsen Honkan|ja|道後温泉本館}}. In a redevelopment in 1973 (Shōwa 48), it was incorporated into the Plaza Five Building, preserving only the original eave ({{ill|hafu|ja|破風}}).{{要出典範囲|The interior was modern and at first resembled a pool, yet the water flowing from the lion-shaped spout retained the characteristic smoothness of Yamaga Onsen.|date=2024-08}} In July 2011 (Heisei 23), redevelopment work on the Plaza Five Building commenced, and in October 2012 (Heisei 24) a restoration of traditional Japanese architecture was completed.{{独自研究範囲|Primarily used by local residents.|date=2024-08}}{{要出典範囲|A drinking facility existed in front of the old building’s entrance, providing potable hot spring water 24 hours a day.|date=2024-08}} == History == === Origins === In ancient times, the {{ill|Wamyo Ruijusho|ja|倭名類聚抄}}, a dictionary from the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, records the place name "Yun" (温泉) in "Yamaga-gun" (山鹿郡). Yamaga Onsen is considered one of the oldest hot spring resorts in Kumamoto Prefecture, and several legends regarding its origin persist. One theory posits that during the {{ill|Hōgen Rebellion|ja|保元の乱}}, after being defeated and retreating from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}}, {{ill|Uno Chika-ji|ja|宇野親治}} was hunting in the mountains and discovered the hot spring upon seeing a herd of {{ill|deer|ja|鹿}} lying in a valley. The name "Yamaga" is said to derive from this {{ill|Hot Spring Discovery Legend|ja|開湯伝説}}. Another tradition holds that a descendant of Kikuchi Noritaka’s second son, Masataka—namely, the progenitor of Yamaga Sogyo, {{ill|Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|ja|山鹿太郎重光}}—relocated people to the area in the 3rd year of the {{ill|Jishō period|ja|治承}} and constructed bathing facilities, thus laying the foundation for the growth of Yamaga town. === Early Modern Period === During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, as a post town, Yamaga Onsen flourished to the extent that it was said there was "not a single one of a thousand houses missing." At the site where Uno Chika-ji discovered the deer, a bathhouse for the feudal lord was established, and an imperial bath ({{ill|Gomae-yu|ja|御前湯}}) was installed. === Modern and Contemporary Periods === In 1923 (Taishō 12), the Yamaga Onsen Railway (later known as the {{ill|Yamaga Onsen Railway|ja|山鹿温泉鉄道}}) began operations, with {{ill|Yamaga Station|ja|山鹿駅}} serving as the nearest station; however, it was discontinued in 1960 (Shōwa 35) due to flooding. In November 2006 (Heisei 18), together with three other hot spring areas in the Kikuchi River basin—{{ill|Tama Onsen|ja|玉名温泉}}, {{ill|Kikuchi Onsen|ja|菊池温泉}}, and {{ill|Ueki Onsen|ja|植木温泉}}—the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort Development Council" was established, aiming to brand the area as the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort" and to develop common products and wide-area tourism routes. == Access == * From the {{ill|Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal|ja|熊本桜町バスターミナル}}, take the {{ill|Kyushu Sankō Bus|ja|九州産交バス}} bound for Yamaga Onsen and alight at "Yamaga Onsen (Yachiyo-za Entrance)." * Approximately 12 km from the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}} {{ill|Ueki Interchange|ja|植木インターチェンジ}}, and 9.5 km from the {{ill|Kikusui Interchange|ja|菊水インターチェンジ}}. == Related Topics == * Hot springs in Yamaga City ** {{ill|Hirayama Onsen|ja|平山温泉}} ** {{ill|Kumairi Onsen|ja|熊入温泉}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Yamaga Onsen}} {{Wiki Portal Link|Hot Springs|[[File:Hot springs 001.svg|34px|Portal: Hot Springs]]}} * [https://www.y-kankoukyoukai.com/ {{ill|Yamaga Onsen Tourism Association|ja|山鹿温泉観光協会}}] * [https://yamaga-tanbou.jp/genre/onsen/ {{ill|Yamaga Onsen|ja|山鹿温泉}}] – {{混同|山香温泉}} {{日本の温泉地 |名称 = 山鹿温泉 |画像 = [[File:Sakura-yu Yamaga ac (1).jpg|280px]]
共同湯の[[さくら湯]] |コメント = |所在地 = [[熊本県]][[山鹿市]]{{日本の位置情報|33|0|53.1|130|41|20.3|山鹿温泉|33.01475,130.688972|山鹿温泉|nocoord=yes}} {{Location map|Japan Kumamoto Prefecture|relief=1|float=center|width=225}} |緯度度 =33 |緯度分 =0 |緯度秒 =53.1 |経度度 =130 |経度分 =41 |経度秒 =20.3 |交通アクセス = ''詳しくは[[#アクセス|アクセス]]の項を参照のこと'' |泉質 = [[単純温泉]] |泉温 = |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = アルカリ性 |浸透圧の分類 = |宿泊施設数 = |総収容人員数 = |年間浴客数 = |統計年度 = |外部リンク = [https://yamaga-tanbou.jp/genre/onsen/ 山鹿市の山鹿温泉の公式サイト] |特記事項 = }} '''山鹿温泉'''（やまがおんせん）は、[[熊本県]][[山鹿市]]（旧国[[肥後国]]）にある[[温泉]]。毎年8月に催される「[[山鹿灯籠まつり]]」で知られる。共同湯（公衆浴場）として[[さくら湯]]がある。 == 泉質 == * アルカリ性単純温泉 非常に滑らかで美肌効果があるといわれる。 == 温泉街 == [[File:yamaga_ashiyu.jpg|thumb|「あし湯」]] [[旅館]]や[[ホテル]]は約20軒であり、熊本県有数の規模を持つ。また、歴史の古い温泉街として風情を強調しており、明治時代には[[道後温泉]]を倣って温泉街を作ったといわれる。市街地の中にありながら、[[露天風呂]]や浴室に工夫が凝らされており、情緒豊かである。湯量も多く、「あし湯」、[[共同浴場]]のほか温泉プール（現存しない）などが設けられた。旧[[豊前街道]]には1910年（明治45年）竣工の芝居小屋である[[八千代座]]がある。 === 共同湯 === 共同湯として[[さくら湯]]がある。元々は[[道後温泉本館]]を建てた棟梁を招いて建築された。 1973年（昭和48年）の再開発によってプラザファイブビルに入り、旧館の[[破風]]のみが保存された。{{要出典範囲|内部は近代的で一見プール状になっていたが、ライオンの注ぎ口から流れ出る湯は山鹿温泉の特徴であるなめらかさを十分に保っていた。|date=2024-08}} 2011年（平成23年）7月にはプラザファイブビルの再開発工事が着工され、2012年（平成24年）10月に和風建築の復元工事が完成した。{{独自研究範囲|主として近隣住民が利用している。|date=2024-08}}{{要出典範囲|旧施設の入口前に[[飲泉]]設備があり、24時間飲用可能な湯が出ている。|date=2024-08}} == 歴史 == === 起源 === 古くは、[[平安時代]]の辞書『[[和名類聚抄|倭名類聚抄]]』に引く「山鹿郡」に「温泉（ユ）」の地名記載がある。 山鹿の温泉は熊本県内で最も歴史のある温泉地の一つと言われ、その起源についてはいくつかの伝説が残されている。一説に、[[保元の乱]]で[[京都]]から敗走した[[宇野親治]]が山狩りをしていたところ、渓谷に[[シカ|鹿]]が群れ臥しているのを見て、この温泉を発見したとされる。「山鹿」の名もこの[[開湯伝説]]に因むという。また、[[菊池則隆]]の次男政隆の末裔にして[[山鹿素行]]の祖先・[[豪族]][[山鹿太郎重光]]が[[治承]]3年にこの地に民を移住させ、浴槽を設けて入浴の便を図った。以来、移り住む者が増えて山鹿の町が出来たとも伝える。 === 近世 === [[宿場町]]としても繁栄した[[江戸時代]]には「山鹿千軒たらい無し」と謳われるほど賑わった。宇野親治が鹿を発見した場所に城主の湯屋が設けられ、[[御前湯]]が置かれた。 === 近現代 === 1923年（大正12年）には鹿本鉄道（後の[[山鹿温泉鉄道]]）が開業し、山鹿温泉の最寄駅として[[山鹿駅]]があったが、[[水害]]によって1960年（昭和35年）に廃線となった。 2006年（平成18年）11月には、菊池川流域の[[玉名温泉]]、[[菊池温泉]]、[[植木温泉]]の3温泉地とともに「菊池川温泉郷づくり協議会」を発足させ、「菊池川温泉郷」ブランド化を目指して共通商品や広域観光ルートの開発などに取り組んでいくことになった。 == アクセス == *[[熊本桜町バスターミナル]]、[[玉名駅]]、[[新玉名駅]]のいずれかから[[九州産交バス]]山鹿温泉ゆきに乗車し「山鹿温泉（八千代座入口）」下車。 *[[九州自動車道]][[植木インターチェンジ]]から12㎞、[[菊水インターチェンジ]]から9.5㎞。 == 周辺施設 == * [[山鹿灯籠民芸館]] * [[八千代座]] * [[さくら湯]] == 関連項目 == * 山鹿市内の温泉 ** [[平山温泉]] ** [[熊入温泉]] == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Yamaga Onsen}} {{ウィキポータルリンク|温泉|[[画像:Hot springs 001.svg|34px|Portal:温泉]]}} * [https://www.y-kankoukyoukai.com/ 山鹿温泉観光協会] * [https://yamaga-tanbou.jp/genre/onsen/ 山鹿温泉] 山鹿市 {{温泉}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やまかおんせん}} [[Category:熊本県の温泉]] [[Category:山鹿市]] {{Onsen-stub}}

{{混同|Yamakawa Onsen}} {{Japanese Hot Spring | Name = Yamaga Onsen (山鹿温泉) | image = [[File:Sakura-yu Yamaga ac (1).jpg|280px]]
Communal bath {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯}} | Comment = | Location = {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}{{ill|Yamaga City|ja|山鹿市}} {{Japanese Location Information|33|0|53.1|130|41|20.3|Yamaga Onsen|33.01475,130.688972|Yamaga Onsen|nocoord=yes}} {{Location map|Japan Kumamoto Prefecture|relief=1|float=center|width=225}} | Latitude Degrees = 33 | Latitude Minutes = 0 | Latitude Seconds = 53.1 | Longitude Degrees = 130 | Longitude Minutes = 41 | Longitude Seconds = 20.3 | Access = ''For details, see the [[#Access|Access]] section.'' | Spring Quality = {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉}} | Spring Temperature = | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = Alkaline | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = [https://yamaga-tanbou.jp/genre/onsen/ Official site of Yamaga Onsen in Yamaga City] | Special Notes = }} '''Yamaga Onsen''' (やまがおんせん) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉}} located in {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}{{ill|Yamaga City|ja|山鹿市}} (formerly part of {{ill|Higo Province|ja|肥後国}}). It is renowned for the annual "Yamaga Lantern Festival" ({{ill|Yamaga Tōrō Matsuri|ja|山鹿灯籠まつり}}) held every August. A communal bath (public bath) known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯}} is available. == Spring Quality == * Alkaline simple hot spring It is reputed for its exceptionally smooth water that is said to have excellent beautifying effects. == Hot Spring Town == [[File:yamaga_ashiyu.jpg|thumb|"Ashiyu"]] There are approximately 20 establishments including {{ill|ryokan|ja|旅館}} and {{ill|hotels|ja|ホテル}}, making it one of the largest hot spring towns in Kumamoto Prefecture. As an old hot spring town, it emphasizes its historic charm, and it is said that during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}} it was modeled after {{ill|Dogo Onsen|ja|道後温泉}}. Although it is located within the urban area, creatively designed open-air baths and bathing rooms enhance its quaint atmosphere. In addition to "Ashiyu" and communal baths, a hot spring pool (now no longer extant) was once established. Along the former {{ill|Buzen Highway|ja|豊前街道}}, there stands the Yachiyo-za, a theater building completed in 1910 (Meiji 45). === Communal Bath === A communal bath known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯}} exists. It was originally constructed by inviting the master carpenter who built the main building of {{ill|Dogo Onsen Honkan|ja|道後温泉本館}}. In a redevelopment in 1973 (Shōwa 48), it was incorporated into the Plaza Five Building, preserving only the original eave ({{ill|hafu|ja|破風}}).{{要出典範囲|The interior was modern and at first resembled a pool, yet the water flowing from the lion-shaped spout retained the characteristic smoothness of Yamaga Onsen.|date=2024-08}} In July 2011 (Heisei 23), redevelopment work on the Plaza Five Building commenced, and in October 2012 (Heisei 24) a restoration of traditional Japanese architecture was completed.{{独自研究範囲|Primarily used by local residents.|date=2024-08}}{{要出典範囲|A drinking facility existed in front of the old building’s entrance, providing potable hot spring water 24 hours a day.|date=2024-08}} == History == === Origins === In ancient times, the {{ill|Wamyo Ruijusho|ja|倭名類聚抄}}, a dictionary from the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, records the place name "Yun" (温泉) in "Yamaga-gun" (山鹿郡). Yamaga Onsen is considered one of the oldest hot spring resorts in Kumamoto Prefecture, and several legends regarding its origin persist. One theory posits that during the {{ill|Hōgen Rebellion|ja|保元の乱}}, after being defeated and retreating from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}}, {{ill|Uno Chika-ji|ja|宇野親治}} was hunting in the mountains and discovered the hot spring upon seeing a herd of {{ill|deer|ja|鹿}} lying in a valley. The name "Yamaga" is said to derive from this {{ill|Hot Spring Discovery Legend|ja|開湯伝説}}. Another tradition holds that a descendant of Kikuchi Noritaka’s second son, Masataka—namely, the progenitor of Yamaga Sogyo, {{ill|Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|ja|山鹿太郎重光}}—relocated people to the area in the 3rd year of the {{ill|Jishō period|ja|治承}} and constructed bathing facilities, thus laying the foundation for the growth of Yamaga town. === Early Modern Period === During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, as a post town, Yamaga Onsen flourished to the extent that it was said there was "not a single one of a thousand houses missing." At the site where Uno Chika-ji discovered the deer, a bathhouse for the feudal lord was established, and an imperial bath ({{ill|Gomae-yu|ja|御前湯}}) was installed. === Modern and Contemporary Periods === In 1923 (Taishō 12), the Yamaga Onsen Railway (later known as the {{ill|Yamaga Onsen Railway|ja|山鹿温泉鉄道}}) began operations, with {{ill|Yamaga Station|ja|山鹿駅}} serving as the nearest station; however, it was discontinued in 1960 (Shōwa 35) due to flooding. In November 2006 (Heisei 18), together with three other hot spring areas in the Kikuchi River basin—{{ill|Tama Onsen|ja|玉名温泉}}, {{ill|Kikuchi Onsen|ja|菊池温泉}}, and {{ill|Ueki Onsen|ja|植木温泉}}—the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort Development Council" was established, aiming to brand the area as the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort" and to develop common products and wide-area tourism routes. == Access == * From the {{ill|Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal|ja|熊本桜町バスターミナル}}, take the {{ill|Kyushu Sankō Bus|ja|九州産交バス}} bound for Yamaga Onsen and alight at "Yamaga Onsen (Yachiyo-za Entrance)." * Approximately 12 km from the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}} {{ill|Ueki Interchange|ja|植木インターチェンジ}}, and 9.5 km from the {{ill|Kikusui Interchange|ja|菊水インターチェンジ}}. == Related Topics == * Hot springs in Yamaga City ** {{ill|Hirayama Onsen|ja|平山温泉}} ** {{ill|Kumairi Onsen|ja|熊入温泉}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Yamaga Onsen}} {{Wiki Portal Link|Hot Springs|[[File:Hot springs 001.svg|34px|Portal: Hot Springs]]}} * [https://www.y-kankoukyoukai.com/ {{ill|Yamaga Onsen Tourism Association|ja|山鹿温泉観光協会}}] * [https://yamaga-tanbou.jp/genre/onsen/ {{ill|Yamaga Onsen|ja|山鹿温泉}}] – Yamaga City {{Hot Spring}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やまかおんせん}} [[Category:Hot Springs in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamaga City]] [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E9%B9%BF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] The name "Yamaga" is said to derive from this {{ill|Hot Spring Discovery Legend|ja|開湯伝説}}. Another tradition holds that a descendant of Kikuchi Noritaka’s second son, Masataka—namely, the progenitor of Yamaga Sogyo, {{ill|Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|ja|山鹿太郎重光}}—relocated people to the area in the 3rd year of the {{ill|Jishō period|ja|治承}} and constructed bathing facilities, thus laying the foundation for the growth of Yamaga town. === Early Modern Period === During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, as a post town, Yamaga Onsen flourished to the extent that it was said there was "not a single one of a thousand houses missing." At the site where Uno Chika-ji discovered the deer, a bathhouse for the feudal lord was established, and an imperial bath ({{ill|Gomae-yu|ja|御前湯}}) was installed. === Modern and Contemporary Periods === In 1923 (Taishō 12), the Yamaga Onsen Railway (later known as the {{ill|Yamaga Onsen Railway|ja|山鹿温泉鉄道}}) began operations, with {{ill|Yamaga Station|ja|山鹿駅}} serving as the nearest station; however, it was discontinued in 1960 (Shōwa 35) due to flooding. In November 2006 (Heisei 18), together with three other hot spring areas in the Kikuchi River basin—{{ill|Tama Onsen|ja|玉名温泉}}, {{ill|Kikuchi Onsen|ja|菊池温泉}}, and {{ill|Ueki Onsen|ja|植木温泉}}—the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort Development Council" was established, aiming to brand the area as the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort" and to develop common products and wide-area tourism routes. == Access == * From the {{ill|Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal|ja|熊本桜町バスターミナル}}, take the {{ill|Kyushu Sankō Bus|ja|九州産交バス}} bound for Yamaga Onsen and alight at "Yamaga Onsen (Yachiyo-za Entrance)." * Approximately 12 km from the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}} {{ill|Ueki Interchange|ja|植木インターチェンジ}}, and 9.5 km from the {{ill|Kikusui Interchange|ja|菊水インターチェンジ}}. == Related Topics == * Hot springs in Yamaga City ** {{ill|Hirayama Onsen|ja|平山温泉}} ** {{ill|Kumairi Onsen|ja|熊入温泉}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Yamaga Onsen}} {{Wiki Portal Link|Hot Springs|[[File:Hot springs 001.svg|34px|Portal: Hot Springs]]}} * [https://www.y-kankoukyoukai.com/ {{ill|Yamaga Onsen Tourism Association|ja|山鹿温泉観光協会}}] * [https://yamaga-tanbou.jp/genre/onsen/ {{ill|Yamaga Onsen|ja|山鹿温泉}}] – Yamaga City {{Hot Spring}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やまかおんせん}} [[Category:Hot Springs in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamaga City]] [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E9%B9%BF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] {{混同|Yamakawa Onsen}} {{Japanese Hot Spring | Name = Yamaga Onsen (山鹿温泉) | image = [[File:Sakura-yu Yamaga ac (1).jpg|280px]]
Communal bath {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯}} | Comment = | Location = {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}{{ill|Yamaga City|ja|山鹿市}} {{Japanese Location Information|33|0|53.1|130|41|20.3|Yamaga Onsen|33.01475,130.688972|Yamaga Onsen|nocoord=yes}} {{Location map|Japan Kumamoto Prefecture|relief=1|float=center|width=225}} | Latitude Degrees = 33 | Latitude Minutes = 0 | Latitude Seconds = 53.1 | Longitude Degrees = 130 | Longitude Minutes = 41 | Longitude Seconds = 20.3 | Access = ''For details, see the [[#Access|Access]] section.'' | Spring Quality = {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉}} | Spring Temperature = | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = Alkaline | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = [https://yamaga-tanbou.jp/genre/onsen/ Official site of Yamaga Onsen in Yamaga City] | Special Notes = }} '''Yamaga Onsen''' (やまがおんせん) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉}} located in {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}{{ill|Yamaga City|ja|山鹿市}} (formerly part of {{ill|Higo Province|ja|肥後国}}). It is renowned for the annual "Yamaga Lantern Festival" ({{ill|Yamaga Tōrō Matsuri|ja|山鹿灯籠まつり}}) held every August. A communal bath (public bath) known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯}} is available. == Spring Quality == * Alkaline simple hot spring It is reputed for its exceptionally smooth water that is said to have excellent beautifying effects. == Hot Spring Town == [[File:yamaga_ashiyu.jpg|thumb|"Ashiyu"]] There are approximately 20 establishments including {{ill|ryokan|ja|旅館}} and {{ill|hotels|ja|ホテル}}, making it one of the largest hot spring towns in Kumamoto Prefecture. As an old hot spring town, it emphasizes its historic charm, and it is said that during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}} it was modeled after {{ill|Dogo Onsen|ja|道後温泉}}. Although it is located within the urban area, creatively designed open-air baths and bathing rooms enhance its quaint atmosphere. In addition to "Ashiyu" and communal baths, a hot spring pool (now no longer extant) was once established. Along the former {{ill|Buzen Highway|ja|豊前街道}}, there stands the Yachiyo-za, a theater building completed in 1910 (Meiji 45). === Communal Bath === A communal bath known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯}} exists. It was originally constructed by inviting the master carpenter who built the main building of {{ill|Dogo Onsen Honkan|ja|道後温泉本館}}. In a redevelopment in 1973 (Shōwa 48), it was incorporated into the Plaza Five Building, preserving only the original eave ({{ill|hafu|ja|破風}}).{{要出典範囲|The interior was modern and at first resembled a pool, yet the water flowing from the lion-shaped spout retained the characteristic smoothness of Yamaga Onsen.|date=2024-08}} In July 2011 (Heisei 23), redevelopment work on the Plaza Five Building commenced, and in October 2012 (Heisei 24) a restoration of traditional Japanese architecture was completed.{{独自研究範囲|Primarily used by local residents.|date=2024-08}}{{要出典範囲|A drinking facility existed in front of the old building’s entrance, providing potable hot spring water 24 hours a day.|date=2024-08}} == History == === Origins === In ancient times, the {{ill|Wamyo Ruijusho|ja|倭名類聚抄}}, a dictionary from the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, records the place name "Yun" (温泉) in "Yamaga-gun" (山鹿郡). Yamaga Onsen is considered one of the oldest hot spring resorts in Kumamoto Prefecture, and several legends regarding its origin persist. One theory posits that during the {{ill|Hōgen Rebellion|ja|保元の乱}}, after being defeated and retreating from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}}, {{ill|Uno Chika-ji|ja|宇野親治}} was hunting in the mountains and discovered the hot spring upon seeing a herd of {{ill|deer|ja|鹿}} lying in a valley. The name "Yamaga" is said to derive from this {{ill|Hot Spring Discovery Legend|ja|開湯伝説}}. Another tradition holds that a descendant of Kikuchi Noritaka’s second son, Masataka—namely, the progenitor of Yamaga Sogyo, {{ill|Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|ja|山鹿太郎重光}}—relocated people to the area in the 3rd year of the {{ill|Jishō period|ja|治承}} and constructed bathing facilities, thus laying the foundation for the growth of Yamaga town. === Early Modern Period === During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, as a post town, Yamaga Onsen flourished to the extent that it was said there was "not a single one of a thousand houses missing." At the site where Uno Chika-ji discovered the deer, a bathhouse for the feudal lord was established, and an imperial bath ({{ill|Gomae-yu|ja|御前湯}}) was installed. === Modern and Contemporary Periods === In 1923 (Taishō 12), the Yamaga Onsen Railway (later known as the {{ill|Yamaga Onsen Railway|ja|山鹿温泉鉄道}}) began operations, with {{ill|Yamaga Station|ja|山鹿駅}} serving as the nearest station; however, it was discontinued in 1960 (Shōwa 35) due to flooding. In November 2006 (Heisei 18), together with three other hot spring areas in the Kikuchi River basin—{{ill|Tama Onsen|ja|玉名温泉}}, {{ill|Kikuchi Onsen|ja|菊池温泉}}, and {{ill|Ueki Onsen|ja|植木温泉}}—the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort Development Council" was established, aiming to brand the area as the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort" and to develop common products and wide-area tourism routes. == Access == * From the {{ill|Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal|ja|熊本桜町バスターミナル}}, take the {{ill|Kyushu Sankō Bus|ja|九州産交バス}} bound for Yamaga Onsen and alight at "Yamaga Onsen (Yachiyo-za Entrance)." * Approximately 12 km from the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}} {{ill|Ueki Interchange|ja|植木インターチェンジ}}, and 9.5 km from the {{ill|Kikusui Interchange|ja|菊水インターチェンジ}}. == Related Topics == * Hot springs in Yamaga City ** {{ill|Hirayama Onsen|ja|平山温泉}} ** {{ill|Kumairi Onsen|ja|熊入温泉}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Yamaga Onsen}} {{Wiki Portal Link|Hot Springs|[[File:Hot springs 001.svg|34px|Portal: Hot Springs]]}} * [https://www.y-kankoukyoukai.com/ {{ill|Yamaga Onsen Tourism Association|ja|山鹿温泉観光協会}}] * [https://yamaga-tanbou.jp/genre/onsen/ {{ill|Yamaga Onsen|ja|山鹿温泉}}] – Along the former {{ill|Buzen Highway|ja|豊前街道}}, there stands the Yachiyo-za, a theater building completed in 1910 (Meiji 45). === Communal Bath === A communal bath known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯}} exists. It was originally constructed by inviting the master carpenter who built the main building of {{ill|Dogo Onsen Honkan|ja|道後温泉本館}}. In a redevelopment in 1973 (Shōwa 48), it was incorporated into the Plaza Five Building, preserving only the original eave ({{ill|hafu|ja|破風}}).{{要出典範囲|The interior was modern and at first resembled a pool, yet the water flowing from the lion-shaped spout retained the characteristic smoothness of Yamaga Onsen.|date=2024-08}} In July 2011 (Heisei 23), redevelopment work on the Plaza Five Building commenced, and in October 2012 (Heisei 24) a restoration of traditional Japanese architecture was completed.{{独自研究範囲|Primarily used by local residents.|date=2024-08}}{{要出典範囲|A drinking facility existed in front of the old building’s entrance, providing potable hot spring water 24 hours a day.|date=2024-08}} == History == === Origins === In ancient times, the {{ill|Wamyo Ruijusho|ja|倭名類聚抄}}, a dictionary from the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, records the place name "Yun" (温泉) in "Yamaga-gun" (山鹿郡). Yamaga Onsen is considered one of the oldest hot spring resorts in Kumamoto Prefecture, and several legends regarding its origin persist. One theory posits that during the {{ill|Hōgen Rebellion|ja|保元の乱}}, after being defeated and retreating from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}}, {{ill|Uno Chika-ji|ja|宇野親治}} was hunting in the mountains and discovered the hot spring upon seeing a herd of {{ill|deer|ja|鹿}} lying in a valley. The name "Yamaga" is said to derive from this {{ill|Hot Spring Discovery Legend|ja|開湯伝説}}. Another tradition holds that a descendant of Kikuchi Noritaka’s second son, Masataka—namely, the progenitor of Yamaga Sogyo, {{ill|Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|ja|山鹿太郎重光}}—relocated people to the area in the 3rd year of the {{ill|Jishō period|ja|治承}} and constructed bathing facilities, thus laying the foundation for the growth of Yamaga town. === Early Modern Period === During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, as a post town, Yamaga Onsen flourished to the extent that it was said there was "not a single one of a thousand houses missing." At the site where Uno Chika-ji discovered the deer, a bathhouse for the feudal lord was established, and an imperial bath ({{ill|Gomae-yu|ja|御前湯}}) was installed. === Modern and Contemporary Periods === In 1923 (Taishō 12), the Yamaga Onsen Railway (later known as the {{ill|Yamaga Onsen Railway|ja|山鹿温泉鉄道}}) began operations, with {{ill|Yamaga Station|ja|山鹿駅}} serving as the nearest station; however, it was discontinued in 1960 (Shōwa 35) due to flooding. In November 2006 (Heisei 18), together with three other hot spring areas in the Kikuchi River basin—{{ill|Tama Onsen|ja|玉名温泉}}, {{ill|Kikuchi Onsen|ja|菊池温泉}}, and {{ill|Ueki Onsen|ja|植木温泉}}—the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort Development Council" was established, aiming to brand the area as the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort" and to develop common products and wide-area tourism routes. == Access == * From the {{ill|Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal|ja|熊本桜町バスターミナル}}, take the {{ill|Kyushu Sankō Bus|ja|九州産交バス}} bound for Yamaga Onsen and alight at "Yamaga Onsen (Yachiyo-za Entrance)." * Approximately 12 km from the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}} {{ill|Ueki Interchange|ja|植木インターチェンジ}}, and 9.5 km from the {{ill|Kikusui Interchange|ja|菊水インターチェンジ}}. == Related Topics == * Hot springs in Yamaga City ** {{ill|Hirayama Onsen|ja|平山温泉}} ** {{ill|Kumairi Onsen|ja|熊入温泉}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Yamaga Onsen}} {{Wiki Portal Link|Hot Springs|[[File:Hot springs 001.svg|34px|Portal: Hot Springs]]}} * [https://www.y-kankoukyoukai.com/ {{ill|Yamaga Onsen Tourism Association|ja|山鹿温泉観光協会}]][[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] * {{混同|Yamakawa Onsen}} {{Japanese Hot Spring | Name = Yamaga Onsen (山鹿温泉) | image = [[File:Sakura-yu Yamaga ac (1).jpg|280px]]
Communal bath {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯}} | Comment = | Location = {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}{{ill|Yamaga City|ja|山鹿市}} {{Japanese Location Information|33|0|53.1|130|41|20.3|Yamaga Onsen|33.01475,130.688972|Yamaga Onsen|nocoord=yes}} {{Location map|Japan Kumamoto Prefecture|relief=1|float=center|width=225}} | Latitude Degrees = 33 | Latitude Minutes = 0 | Latitude Seconds = 53.1 | Longitude Degrees = 130 | Longitude Minutes = 41 | Longitude Seconds = 20.3 | Access = ''For details, see the [[#Access|Access]] section.'' | Spring Quality = {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉}} | Spring Temperature = | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = Alkaline | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = [https://yamaga-tanbou.jp/genre/onsen/ Official site of Yamaga Onsen in Yamaga City] | Special Notes = }} '''Yamaga Onsen''' (やまがおんせん) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉}} located in {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}{{ill|Yamaga City|ja|山鹿市}} (formerly part of {{ill|Higo Province|ja|肥後国}}). It is renowned for the annual "Yamaga Lantern Festival" ({{ill|Yamaga Tōrō Matsuri|ja|山鹿灯籠まつり}}) held every August. A communal bath (public bath) known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯}} is available. == Spring Quality == * Alkaline simple hot spring It is reputed for its exceptionally smooth water that is said to have excellent beautifying effects. == Hot Spring Town == [[File:yamaga_ashiyu.jpg|thumb|"Ashiyu"]] There are approximately 20 establishments including {{ill|ryokan|ja|旅館}} and {{ill|hotels|ja|ホテル}}, making it one of the largest hot spring towns in Kumamoto Prefecture. As an old hot spring town, it emphasizes its historic charm, and it is said that during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}} it was modeled after {{ill|Dogo Onsen|ja|道後温泉}}. Although it is located within the urban area, creatively designed open-air baths and bathing rooms enhance its quaint atmosphere. In addition to "Ashiyu" and communal baths, a hot spring pool (now no longer extant) was once established. Along the former {{ill|Buzen Highway|ja|豊前街道}}, there stands the Yachiyo-za, a theater building completed in 1910 (Meiji 45). === Communal Bath === A communal bath known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯}} exists. It was originally constructed by inviting the master carpenter who built the main building of {{ill|Dogo Onsen Honkan|ja|道後温泉本館}}. In a redevelopment in 1973 (Shōwa 48), it was incorporated into the Plaza Five Building, preserving only the original eave ({{ill|hafu|ja|破風}}).{{要出典範囲|The interior was modern and at first resembled a pool, yet the water flowing from the lion-shaped spout retained the characteristic smoothness of Yamaga Onsen.|date=2024-08}} In July 2011 (Heisei 23), redevelopment work on the Plaza Five Building commenced, and in October 2012 (Heisei 24) a restoration of traditional Japanese architecture was completed.{{独自研究範囲|Primarily used by local residents.|date=2024-08}}{{要出典範囲|A drinking facility existed in front of the old building’s entrance, providing potable hot spring water 24 hours a day.|date=2024-08}} == History == === Origins === In ancient times, the {{ill|Wamyo Ruijusho|ja|倭名類聚抄}}, a dictionary from the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, records the place name "Yun" (温泉) in "Yamaga-gun" (山鹿郡). Communal bath {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯}} | Comment = | Location = {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}{{ill|Yamaga City|ja|山鹿市}} {{Japanese Location Information|33|0|53.1|130|41|20.3|Yamaga Onsen|33.01475,130.688972|Yamaga Onsen|nocoord=yes}} {{Location map|Japan Kumamoto Prefecture|relief=1|float=center|width=225}} | Latitude Degrees = 33 | Latitude Minutes = 0 | Latitude Seconds = 53.1 | Longitude Degrees = 130 | Longitude Minutes = 41 | Longitude Seconds = 20.3 | Access = ''For details, see the [[#Access|Access]] section.'' | Spring Quality = {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉}} | Spring Temperature = | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = Alkaline | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = [https://yamaga-tanbou.jp/genre/onsen/ Official site of Yamaga Onsen in Yamaga City] | Special Notes = }} '''Yamaga Onsen''' (やまがおんせん) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉}} located in {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}{{ill|Yamaga City|ja|山鹿市}} (formerly part of {{ill|Higo Province|ja|肥後国}}). It is renowned for the annual "Yamaga Lantern Festival" ({{ill|Yamaga Tōrō Matsuri|ja|山鹿灯籠まつり}}) held every August. A communal bath (public bath) known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯}} is available. == Spring Quality == * Alkaline simple hot spring It is reputed for its exceptionally smooth water that is said to have excellent beautifying effects. == Hot Spring Town == [[File:yamaga_ashiyu.jpg|thumb|"Ashiyu"]] There are approximately 20 establishments including {{ill|ryokan|ja|旅館}} and {{ill|hotels|ja|ホテル}}, making it one of the largest hot spring towns in Kumamoto Prefecture. As an old hot spring town, it emphasizes its historic charm, and it is said that during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}} it was modeled after {{ill|Dogo Onsen|ja|道後温泉}}. Although it is located within the urban area, creatively designed open-air baths and bathing rooms enhance its quaint atmosphere. In addition to "Ashiyu" and communal baths, a hot spring pool (now no longer extant) was once established. Along the former {{ill|Buzen Highway|ja|豊前街道}}, there stands the Yachiyo-za, a theater building completed in 1910 (Meiji 45). === Communal Bath === A communal bath known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯}} exists. It was originally constructed by inviting the master carpenter who built the main building of {{ill|Dogo Onsen Honkan|ja|道後温泉本館}}. In a redevelopment in 1973 (Shōwa 48), it was incorporated into the Plaza Five Building, preserving only the original eave ({{ill|hafu|ja|破風}}).{{要出典範囲|The interior was modern and at first resembled a pool, yet the water flowing from the lion-shaped spout retained the characteristic smoothness of Yamaga Onsen.|date=2024-08}} In July 2011 (Heisei 23), redevelopment work on the Plaza Five Building commenced, and in October 2012 (Heisei 24) a restoration of traditional Japanese architecture was completed.{{独自研究範囲|Primarily used by local residents.|date=2024-08}}{{要出典範囲|A drinking facility existed in front of the old building’s entrance, providing potable hot spring water 24 hours a day.|date=2024-08}} == History == === Origins === In ancient times, the {{ill|Wamyo Ruijusho|ja|倭名類聚抄}}, a dictionary from the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, records the place name "Yun" (温泉) in "Yamaga-gun" (山鹿郡). Yamaga Onsen is considered one of the oldest hot spring resorts in Kumamoto Prefecture, and several legends regarding its origin persist. One theory posits that during the {{ill|Hōgen Rebellion|ja|保元の乱}}, after being defeated and retreating from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}}, {{ill|Uno Chika-ji|ja|宇野親治}} was hunting in the mountains and discovered the hot spring upon seeing a herd of {{ill|deer|ja|鹿}} lying in a valley. The name "Yamaga" is said to derive from this {{ill|Hot Spring Discovery Legend|ja|開湯伝説}}. Another tradition holds that a descendant of Kikuchi Noritaka’s second son, Masataka—namely, the progenitor of Yamaga Sogyo, {{ill|Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|ja|山鹿太郎重光}}—relocated people to the area in the 3rd year of the {{ill|Jishō period|ja|治承}} and constructed bathing facilities, thus laying the foundation for the growth of Yamaga town. === Early Modern Period === During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, as a post town, Yamaga Onsen flourished to the extent that it was said there was "not a single one of a thousand houses missing." At the site where Uno Chika-ji discovered the deer, a bathhouse for the feudal lord was established, and an imperial bath ({{ill|Gomae-yu|ja|御前湯}}) was installed. === Modern and Contemporary Periods === In 1923 (Taishō 12), the Yamaga Onsen Railway (later known as the {{ill|Yamaga Onsen Railway|ja|山鹿温泉鉄道}}) began operations, with {{ill|Yamaga Station|ja|山鹿駅}} serving as the nearest station; however, it was discontinued in 1960 (Shōwa 35) due to flooding. In November 2006 (Heisei 18), together with three other hot spring areas in the Kikuchi River basin—{{ill|Tama Onsen|ja|玉名温泉}}, {{ill|Kikuchi Onsen|ja|菊池温泉}}, and {{ill|Ueki Onsen|ja|植木温泉}}—the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort Development Council" was established, aiming to brand the area as the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort" and to develop common products and wide-area tourism routes. == Access == * From the {{ill|Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal|ja|熊本桜町バスターミナル}}, take the {{ill|Kyushu Sankō Bus|ja|九州産交バス}} bound for Yamaga Onsen and alight at "Yamaga Onsen (Yachiyo-za Entrance)." * Approximately 12 km from the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}} {{ill|Ueki Interchange|ja|植木インターチェンジ}}, and 9.5 km from the {{ill|Kikusui Interchange|ja|菊水インターチェンジ}}. == Related Topics == * Hot springs in Yamaga City ** {{ill|Hirayama Onsen|ja|平山温泉}} ** {{ill|Kumairi Onsen|ja|熊入温泉}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Yamaga Onsen}} {{Wiki Portal Link|Hot Springs|[[File:Hot springs 001.svg|34px|Portal: Hot Springs]]}} * [https://www.y-kankoukyoukai.com/ {{ill|Yamaga Onsen Tourism Association|ja|山鹿温泉観光協会}]][[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] * {{混同|Yamakawa Onsen}} {{Japanese Hot Spring | Name = Yamaga Onsen (山鹿温泉) | image = [[File:Sakura-yu Yamaga ac (1).jpg|280px]]
Communal bath {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯}} | Comment = | Location = {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}{{ill|Yamaga City|ja|山鹿市}} {{Japanese Location Information|33|0|53.1|130|41|20.3|Yamaga Onsen|33.01475,130.688972|Yamaga Onsen|nocoord=yes}} {{Location map|Japan Kumamoto Prefecture|relief=1|float=center|width=225}} | Latitude Degrees = 33 | Latitude Minutes = 0 | Latitude Seconds = 53.1 | Longitude Degrees = 130 | Longitude Minutes = 41 | Longitude Seconds = 20.3 | Access = ''For details, see the [[#Access|Access]] section.'' | Spring Quality = {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs}} | Spring Temperature = | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = Alkaline | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = [https://yamaga-tanbou.jp/genre/onsen/ Official site of Yamaga Onsen in Yamaga City] | Special Notes = }} '''Yamaga Onsen''' (やまがおんせん) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉}} located in {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}{{ill|Yamaga City|ja|山鹿市}} (formerly part of {{ill|Higo Province|ja|肥後国}}). It is renowned for the annual "Yamaga Lantern Festival" ({{ill|Yamaga Tōrō Matsuri|ja|山鹿灯籠まつり}}) held every August. A communal bath (public bath) known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯}} is available. == Spring Quality == * Alkaline simple hot spring It is reputed for its exceptionally smooth water that is said to have excellent beautifying effects. == Hot Spring Town == [[File:yamaga_ashiyu.jpg|thumb|"Ashiyu"]] There are approximately 20 establishments including {{ill|ryokan|ja|旅館}} and {{ill|hotels|ja|ホテル}}, making it one of the largest hot spring towns in Kumamoto Prefecture. As an old hot spring town, it emphasizes its historic charm, and it is said that during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}} it was modeled after {{ill|Dogo Onsen|ja|道後温泉}}. Although it is located within the urban area, creatively designed open-air baths and bathing rooms enhance its quaint atmosphere. In addition to "Ashiyu" and communal baths, a hot spring pool (now no longer extant) was once established. Along the former {{ill|Buzen Highway|ja|豊前街道}}, there stands the Yachiyo-za, a theater building completed in 1910 (Meiji 45). === Communal Bath === A communal bath known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯}} exists. It was originally constructed by inviting the master carpenter who built the main building of {{ill|Dogo Onsen Honkan|ja|道後温泉本館}}. In a redevelopment in 1973 (Shōwa 48), it was incorporated into the Plaza Five Building, preserving only the original eave ({{ill|hafu|ja|破風}}).{{要出典範囲|The interior was modern and at first resembled a pool, yet the water flowing from the lion-shaped spout retained the characteristic smoothness of Yamaga Onsen.|date=2024-08}} In July 2011 (Heisei 23), redevelopment work on the Plaza Five Building commenced, and in October 2012 (Heisei 24) a restoration of traditional Japanese architecture was completed.{{独自研究範囲|Primarily used by local residents.|date=2024-08}}{{要出典範囲|A drinking facility existed in front of the old building’s entrance, providing potable hot spring water 24 hours a day.|date=2024-08}} == History == === Origins === In ancient times, the {{ill|Wamyo Ruijusho|ja|倭名類聚抄}}, a dictionary from the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, records the place name "Yun" (温泉) in "Yamaga-gun" (山鹿郡). Yamaga Onsen is considered one of the oldest hot spring resorts in Kumamoto Prefecture, and several legends regarding its origin persist. One theory posits that during the {{ill|Hōgen Rebellion|ja|保元の乱}}, after being defeated and retreating from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}}, {{ill|Uno Chika-ji|ja|宇野親治}} was hunting in the mountains and discovered the hot spring upon seeing a herd of {{ill|deer|ja|鹿}} lying in a valley. The name "Yamaga" is said to derive from this {{ill|Hot Spring Discovery Legend|ja|開湯伝説}}. Another tradition holds that a descendant of Kikuchi Noritaka’s second son, Masataka—namely, the progenitor of Yamaga Sogyo, {{ill|Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|ja|山鹿太郎重光}}—relocated people to the area in the 3rd year of the {{ill|Jishō period|ja|治承}} and constructed bathing facilities, thus laying the foundation for the growth of Yamaga town. === Early Modern Period === During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, as a post town, Yamaga Onsen flourished to the extent that it was said there was "not a single one of a thousand houses missing." At the site where Uno Chika-ji discovered the deer, a bathhouse for the feudal lord was established, and an imperial bath ({{ill|Gomae-yu|ja|御前湯}}) was installed. === Modern and Contemporary Periods === In 1923 (Taishō 12), the Yamaga Onsen Railway (later known as the {{ill|Yamaga Onsen Railway|ja|山鹿温泉鉄道}}) began operations, with {{ill|Yamaga Station|ja|山鹿駅}} serving as the nearest station; however, it was discontinued in 1960 (Shōwa 35) due to flooding. In November 2006 (Heisei 18), together with three other hot spring areas in the Kikuchi River basin—{{ill|Tama Onsen|ja|玉名温泉}}, {{ill|Kikuchi Onsen|ja|菊池温泉}}, and {{ill|Ueki Onsen|ja|植木温泉}}—the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort Development Council" was established, aiming to brand the area as the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort" and to develop common products and wide-area tourism routes. == Access == * From the {{ill|Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal|ja|熊本桜町バスターミナル}}, take the {{ill|Kyushu Sankō Bus|ja|九州産交バス}} bound for Yamaga Onsen and alight at "Yamaga Onsen (Yachiyo-za Entrance)." * Approximately 12 km from the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}} {{ill|Ueki Interchange|ja|植木インターチェンジ}}, and 9.5 km from the {{ill|Kikusui Interchange|ja|菊水インターチェンジ}}. == Related Topics == * Hot springs in Yamaga City ** {{ill|Hirayama Onsen|ja|平山温泉}} ** {{ill|Kumairi Onsen|ja|熊入温泉}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Yamaga Onsen}} {{Wiki Portal Link|Hot Springs|[[File:Hot springs 001.svg|34px|Portal: Hot Springs]]}} * [https://www.y-kankoukyoukai.com/ {{ill|Yamaga Onsen Tourism Association|ja|山鹿温泉観光協会}]][[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] * [https://yamaga-tanbou.jp/genre/onsen/ {{ill|Yamaga Onsen|ja|山鹿温泉}}] – Yamaga City {{Hot Spring}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やまかおんせん}} [[Category:Hot Springs in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamaga City]] revision.comment: removing the duplicate onsen templates and moving them all to the bottom
revision.text:
{{混同|Yamakawa Onsen}} {{infobox Onsen | Name = Yamaga Onsen (山鹿温泉) | image = [[File:Sakura-yu Yamaga ac (1).jpg|280px]]
Communal bath {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯}} | Comment = | Location = {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}{{ill|Yamaga City|ja|山鹿市}} {{Japanese Location Information|33|0|53.1|130|41|20.3|Yamaga Onsen|33.01475,130.688972|Yamaga Onsen|nocoord=yes}} {{Location map|Japan Kumamoto Prefecture|relief=1|float=center|width=225}} | Latitude Degrees = 33 | Latitude Minutes = 0 | Latitude Seconds = 53.1 | Longitude Degrees = 130 | Longitude Minutes = 41 | Longitude Seconds = 20.3 | Access = ''For details, see the [[#Access|Access]] section.'' | Spring Quality = {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs}} | Spring Temperature = | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = Alkaline | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = [https://yamaga-tanbou.jp/genre/onsen/ Official site of Yamaga Onsen in Yamaga City] | Special Notes = }} '''Yamaga Onsen''' (やまがおんせん) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉}} located in {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}{{ill|Yamaga City|ja|山鹿市}} (formerly part of {{ill|Higo Province|ja|肥後国}}). It is renowned for the annual "Yamaga Lantern Festival" ({{ill|Yamaga Tōrō Matsuri|ja|山鹿灯籠まつり}}) held every August. A communal bath (public bath) known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯}} is available. == Spring Quality == * Alkaline simple hot spring It is reputed for its exceptionally smooth water that is said to have excellent beautifying effects. == Hot Spring Town == [[File:yamaga_ashiyu.jpg|thumb|"Ashiyu"]] There are approximately 20 establishments including {{ill|ryokan|ja|旅館}} and {{ill|hotels|ja|ホテル}}, making it one of the largest hot spring towns in Kumamoto Prefecture. As an old hot spring town, it emphasizes its historic charm, and it is said that during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}} it was modeled after {{ill|Dogo Onsen|ja|道後温泉}}. Although it is located within the urban area, creatively designed open-air baths and bathing rooms enhance its quaint atmosphere. In addition to "Ashiyu" and communal baths, a hot spring pool (now no longer extant) was once established. Along the former {{ill|Buzen Highway|ja|豊前街道}}, there stands the Yachiyo-za, a theater building completed in 1910 (Meiji 45). === Communal Bath === A communal bath known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯}} exists. It was originally constructed by inviting the master carpenter who built the main building of {{ill|Dogo Onsen Honkan|ja|道後温泉本館}}. In a redevelopment in 1973 (Shōwa 48), it was incorporated into the Plaza Five Building, preserving only the original eave ({{ill|hafu|ja|破風}}).{{要出典範囲|The interior was modern and at first resembled a pool, yet the water flowing from the lion-shaped spout retained the characteristic smoothness of Yamaga Onsen.|date=2024-08}} In July 2011 (Heisei 23), redevelopment work on the Plaza Five Building commenced, and in October 2012 (Heisei 24) a restoration of traditional Japanese architecture was completed.{{独自研究範囲|Primarily used by local residents.|date=2024-08}}{{要出典範囲|A drinking facility existed in front of the old building’s entrance, providing potable hot spring water 24 hours a day.|date=2024-08}} == History == === Origins === In ancient times, the {{ill|Wamyo Ruijusho|ja|倭名類聚抄}}, a dictionary from the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, records the place name "Yun" (温泉) in "Yamaga-gun" (山鹿郡). Yamaga Onsen is considered one of the oldest hot spring resorts in Kumamoto Prefecture, and several legends regarding its origin persist. One theory posits that during the {{ill|Hōgen Rebellion|ja|保元の乱}}, after being defeated and retreating from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}}, {{ill|Uno Chika-ji|ja|宇野親治}} was hunting in the mountains and discovered the hot spring upon seeing a herd of {{ill|deer|ja|鹿}} lying in a valley. The name "Yamaga" is said to derive from this {{ill|Hot Spring Discovery Legend|ja|開湯伝説}}. Another tradition holds that a descendant of Kikuchi Noritaka’s second son, Masataka—namely, the progenitor of Yamaga Sogyo, {{ill|Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|ja|山鹿太郎重光}}—relocated people to the area in the 3rd year of the {{ill|Jishō period|ja|治承}} and constructed bathing facilities, thus laying the foundation for the growth of Yamaga town. === Early Modern Period === During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, as a post town, Yamaga Onsen flourished to the extent that it was said there was "not a single one of a thousand houses missing." At the site where Uno Chika-ji discovered the deer, a bathhouse for the feudal lord was established, and an imperial bath ({{ill|Gomae-yu|ja|御前湯}}) was installed. === Modern and Contemporary Periods === In 1923 (Taishō 12), the Yamaga Onsen Railway (later known as the {{ill|Yamaga Onsen Railway|ja|山鹿温泉鉄道}}) began operations, with {{ill|Yamaga Station|ja|山鹿駅}} serving as the nearest station; however, it was discontinued in 1960 (Shōwa 35) due to flooding. In November 2006 (Heisei 18), together with three other hot spring areas in the Kikuchi River basin—{{ill|Tama Onsen|ja|玉名温泉}}, {{ill|Kikuchi Onsen|ja|菊池温泉}}, and {{ill|Ueki Onsen|ja|植木温泉}}—the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort Development Council" was established, aiming to brand the area as the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort" and to develop common products and wide-area tourism routes. == Access == * From the {{ill|Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal|ja|熊本桜町バスターミナル}}, take the {{ill|Kyushu Sankō Bus|ja|九州産交バス}} bound for Yamaga Onsen and alight at "Yamaga Onsen (Yachiyo-za Entrance)." * Approximately 12 km from the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}} {{ill|Ueki Interchange|ja|植木インターチェンジ}}, and 9.5 km from the {{ill|Kikusui Interchange|ja|菊水インターチェンジ}}. == Related Topics == * Hot springs in Yamaga City ** {{ill|Hirayama Onsen|ja|平山温泉}} ** {{ill|Kumairi Onsen|ja|熊入温泉}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Yamaga Onsen}} {{Wiki Portal Link|Hot Springs|[[File:Hot springs 001.svg|34px|Portal: Hot Springs]]}} * [https://www.y-kankoukyoukai.com/ {{ill|Yamaga Onsen Tourism Association|ja|山鹿温泉観光協会}]][[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] * [https://yamaga-tanbou.jp/genre/onsen/ {{ill|Yamaga Onsen|ja|山鹿温泉}}] – Yamaga City {{DEFAULTSORT:やまかおんせん}} [[Category:Hot Springs in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamaga City]] One theory posits that during the {{ill|Hōgen Rebellion|ja|保元の乱}}, after being defeated and retreating from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都}}, {{ill|Uno Chika-ji|ja|宇野親治}} was hunting in the mountains and discovered the hot spring upon seeing a herd of {{ill|deer|ja|鹿}} lying in a valley. The name "Yamaga" is said to derive from this {{ill|Hot Spring Discovery Legend|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Legends}}. Another tradition holds that a descendant of Kikuchi Noritaka’s second son, Masataka—namely, the progenitor of Yamaga Sogyo, {{ill|Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|ja|山鹿太郎重光}}—relocated people to the area in the 3rd year of the {{ill|Jishō period|ja|治承}} and constructed bathing facilities, thus laying the foundation for the growth of Yamaga town. === Early Modern Period === During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, as a post town, Yamaga Onsen flourished to the extent that it was said there was "not a single one of a thousand houses missing." At the site where Uno Chika-ji discovered the deer, a bathhouse for the feudal lord was established, and an imperial bath ({{ill|Gomae-yu|ja|御前湯}}) was installed. === Modern and Contemporary Periods === In 1923 (Taishō 12), the Yamaga Onsen Railway (later known as the {{ill|Yamaga Onsen Railway|ja|山鹿温泉鉄道}}) began operations, with {{ill|Yamaga Station|ja|山鹿駅}} serving as the nearest station; however, it was discontinued in 1960 (Shōwa 35) due to flooding. In November 2006 (Heisei 18), together with three other hot spring areas in the Kikuchi River basin—{{ill|Tama Onsen|ja|玉名温泉}}, {{ill|Kikuchi Onsen|ja|菊池温泉}}, and {{ill|Ueki Onsen|ja|植木温泉}}—the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort Development Council" was established, aiming to brand the area as the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort" and to develop common products and wide-area tourism routes. == Access == * From the {{ill|Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal|ja|熊本桜町バスターミナル}}, take the {{ill|Kyushu Sankō Bus|ja|九州産交バス}} bound for Yamaga Onsen and alight at "Yamaga Onsen (Yachiyo-za Entrance)." * Approximately 12 km from the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}} {{ill|Ueki Interchange|ja|植木インターチェンジ}}, and 9.5 km from the {{ill|Kikusui Interchange|ja|菊水インターチェンジ}}. == Related Topics == * Hot springs in Yamaga City ** {{ill|Hirayama Onsen|ja|平山温泉}} ** {{ill|Kumairi Onsen|ja|熊入温泉}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Yamaga Onsen}} {{Wiki Portal Link|Hot Springs|[[File:Hot springs 001.svg|34px|Portal: Hot Springs]]}} * [https://www.y-kankoukyoukai.com/ {{ill|Yamaga Onsen Tourism Association|ja|山鹿温泉観光協会}]][[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] * [https://yamaga-tanbou.jp/genre/onsen/ {{ill|Yamaga Onsen|ja|山鹿温泉}}] – Yamaga City {{Onsen}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やまかおんせん}} [[Category:Hot Springs in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Yamaga City]]
