Communal bath {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura-yu|qq=}} | Comment = | Location = {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumamoto Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Yamaga City|ja|山鹿市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga City|qq=}} {{Japanese Location Information|33|0|53.1|130|41|20.3|Yamaga Onsen|33.01475,130.688972|Yamaga Onsen|nocoord=yes}} {{Location map|Japan Kumamoto Prefecture|relief=1|float=center|width=225}} | Latitude Degrees = 33 | Latitude Minutes = 0 | Latitude Seconds = 53.1 | Longitude Degrees = 130 | Longitude Minutes = 41 | Longitude Seconds = 20.3 | Access = ''For details, see the [[#Access|Access]] section.'' | Spring Quality = {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} | Spring Temperature = | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = Alkaline | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = [https://yamaga-tanbou.jp/genre/onsen/ Official site of Yamaga Onsen in Yamaga City] | Special Notes = }} '''Yamaga Onsen''' (やまがおんせん) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|温泉|1=Onsen|1=Onsen|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hot spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen|qq=}} located in {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumamoto Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Yamaga City|ja|山鹿市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga City|qq=}} (formerly part of {{ill|Higo Province|ja|肥後国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higo Province|qq=}}). It is renowned for the annual "Yamaga Lantern Festival" ({{ill|Yamaga Tōrō Matsuri|ja|山鹿灯籠まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Tōrō Matsuri|qq=}}) held every August. A communal bath (public bath) known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura-yu|qq=}} is available. == Spring Quality == * Alkaline simple hot spring It is reputed for its exceptionally smooth water that is said to have excellent beautifying effects. == Hot Spring Town == [[File:yamaga_ashiyu.jpg|thumb|"Ashiyu"]] There are approximately 20 establishments including {{ill|ryokan|ja|旅館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ryokan|qq=}} and {{ill|hotels|ja|ホテル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hotels|qq=}}, making it one of the largest hot spring towns in Kumamoto Prefecture. As an old hot spring town, it emphasizes its historic charm, and it is said that during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|2=ja|3=明治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}} it was modeled after {{ill|Dogo Onsen|ja|道後温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dogo Onsen|qq=}}. Although it is located within the urban area, creatively designed open-air baths and bathing rooms enhance its quaint atmosphere. In addition to "Ashiyu" and communal baths, a hot spring pool (now no longer extant) was once established. Along the former {{ill|Buzen Highway|ja|豊前街道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buzen Highway|qq=}}, there stands the Yachiyo-za, a theater building completed in 1910 (Meiji 45). === Communal Bath === A communal bath known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura-yu|qq=}} exists. It was originally constructed by inviting the master carpenter who built the main building of {{ill|Dogo Onsen Honkan|ja|道後温泉本館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dogo Onsen Honkan|qq=}}. In a redevelopment in 1973 (Shōwa 48), it was incorporated into the Plaza Five Building, preserving only the original eave ({{ill|hafu|ja|破風|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hafu|qq=}}).{{Citation needed範囲|The interior was modern and at first resembled a pool, yet the water flowing from the lion-shaped spout retained the characteristic smoothness of Yamaga Onsen.|date=2024-08}} In July 2011 (Heisei 23), redevelopment work on the Plaza Five Building commenced, and in October 2012 (Heisei 24) a restoration of traditional Japanese architecture was completed.{{独自研究範囲|Primarily used by local residents.|date=2024-08}}{{Citation needed範囲|A drinking facility existed in front of the old building’s entrance, providing potable hot spring water 24 hours a day.|date=2024-08}} == History == === Origins === In ancient times, the {{ill|Wamyo Ruijusho|ja|倭名類聚抄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wamyo Ruijusho|qq=}}, a dictionary from the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}}, records the place name "Yun" (温泉) in "Yamaga-gun" (山鹿郡). Yamaga Onsen is considered one of the oldest hot spring resorts in Kumamoto Prefecture, and several legends regarding its origin persist. One theory posits that during the {{ill|Hōgen Rebellion|ja|保元の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōgen Rebellion|qq=}}, after being defeated and retreating from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Uno Chika-ji|ja|宇野親治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uno Chika-ji|qq=}} was hunting in the mountains and discovered the hot spring upon seeing a herd of {{ill|deer|ja|鹿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/deer|qq=}} lying in a valley. The name "Yamaga" is said to derive from this {{ill|Hot Spring Discovery Legend|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Discovery Legend|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}. Another tradition holds that a descendant of Kikuchi Noritaka’s second son, Masataka—namely, the progenitor of Yamaga Sogyo, {{ill|Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|ja|山鹿太郎重光|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|qq=}}—relocated people to the area in the 3rd year of the {{ill|Jishō period|ja|治承|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jishō period|qq=}} and constructed bathing facilities, thus laying the foundation for the growth of Yamaga town. === Early Modern Period === During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, as a post town, Yamaga Onsen flourished to the extent that it was said there was "not a single one of a thousand houses missing." At the site where Uno Chika-ji discovered the deer, a bathhouse for the feudal lord was established, and an imperial bath ({{ill|Gomae-yu|ja|御前湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gomae-yu|qq=}}) was installed. === Modern and Contemporary Periods === In 1923 (Taishō 12), the Yamaga Onsen Railway (later known as the {{ill|Yamaga Onsen Railway|ja|山鹿温泉鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Onsen Railway|qq=}}) began operations, with {{ill|Yamaga Station|ja|山鹿駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Station|qq=}} serving as the nearest station; however, it was discontinued in 1960 (Shōwa 35) due to flooding. In November 2006 (Heisei 18), together with three other hot spring areas in the Kikuchi River basin—{{ill|Tama Onsen|ja|玉名温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tama Onsen|qq=}}, {{ill|Kikuchi Onsen|ja|菊池温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Onsen|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ueki Onsen|ja|植木温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueki Onsen|qq=}}—the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort Development Council" was established, aiming to brand the area as the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort" and to develop common products and wide-area tourism routes. == Access == * From the {{ill|Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal|ja|熊本桜町バスターミナル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal|qq=}}, take the {{ill|Kyushu Sankō Bus|ja|九州産交バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu Sankō Bus|qq=}} bound for Yamaga Onsen and alight at "Yamaga Onsen (Yachiyo-za Entrance)." * Approximately 12 km from the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu Expressway|qq=}} {{ill|Ueki Interchange|ja|植木インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueki Interchange|qq=}}, and 9.5 km from the {{ill|Kikusui Interchange|ja|菊水インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikusui Interchange|qq=}}. == Related Topics == * Hot springs in Yamaga City ** {{ill|Hirayama Onsen|ja|平山温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirayama Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Kumairi Onsen|ja|熊入温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumairi Onsen|qq=}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Yamaga Onsen}} {{Wiki Portal Link|Hot Springs|[[File:Hot springs 001.svg|34px|Portal: Hot Springs]]}} * [https://www.y-kankoukyoukai.com/ {{ill|Yamaga Onsen Tourism Association|ja|山鹿温泉観光協会}]] * [https://yamaga-tanbou.jp/genre/onsen/ {{ill|Yamaga Onsen|ja|山鹿温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Onsen|qq=}}] – Yamaga City {{Onsen}} {{混同|Yamakawa Onsen}} {{infobox Onsen | Name = Yamaga Onsen (山鹿温泉) | image = [[File:Sakura-yu Yamaga ac (1).jpg|280px]]
Communal bath {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura-yu|qq=}} | Comment = | Location = [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]][[:en:Yamaga, Kumamoto|Yamaga City]] {{Japanese Location Information|33|0|53.1|130|41|20.3|Yamaga Onsen|33.01475,130.688972|Yamaga Onsen|nocoord=yes}} {{Location map|Japan Kumamoto Prefecture|relief=1|float=center|width=225}} | Latitude Degrees = 33 | Latitude Minutes = 0 | Latitude Seconds = 53.1 | Longitude Degrees = 130 | Longitude Minutes = 41 | Longitude Seconds = 20.3 | Access = ''For details, see the [[#Access|Access]] section.'' | Spring Quality = {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} | Spring Temperature = | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = Alkaline | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = [https://yamaga-tanbou.jp/genre/onsen/ Official site of Yamaga Onsen in Yamaga City] | Special Notes = }} '''Yamaga Onsen''' (やまがおんせん) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]][[:en:Yamaga, Kumamoto|Yamaga City]] (formerly part of [[:en:Higo Province|Higo Province]]). It is renowned for the annual "Yamaga Lantern Festival" ({{ill|Yamaga Tōrō Matsuri|ja|山鹿灯籠まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Tōrō Matsuri|qq=}}) held every August. A communal bath (public bath) known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura-yu|qq=}} is available. == Spring Quality == * Alkaline simple hot spring It is reputed for its exceptionally smooth water that is said to have excellent beautifying effects. == Hot Spring Town == [[File:yamaga_ashiyu.jpg|thumb|"Ashiyu"]] There are approximately 20 establishments including [[:en:Ryokan|ryokan]] and [[:en:Hotel|hotels]], making it one of the largest hot spring towns in Kumamoto Prefecture. As an old hot spring town, it emphasizes its historic charm, and it is said that during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] it was modeled after [[:en:Dōgo Onsen|Dogo Onsen]]. Although it is located within the urban area, creatively designed open-air baths and bathing rooms enhance its quaint atmosphere. In addition to "Ashiyu" and communal baths, a hot spring pool (now no longer extant) was once established. Along the former {{ill|Buzen Highway|ja|豊前街道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buzen Highway|qq=}}, there stands the Yachiyo-za, a theater building completed in 1910 (Meiji 45). === Communal Bath === A communal bath known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura-yu|qq=}} exists. It was originally constructed by inviting the master carpenter who built the main building of {{ill|Dogo Onsen Honkan|ja|道後温泉本館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dogo Onsen Honkan|qq=}}. In a redevelopment in 1973 (Shōwa 48), it was incorporated into the Plaza Five Building, preserving only the original eave ([[:en:Bargeboard|hafu]]).{{Citation needed範囲|The interior was modern and at first resembled a pool, yet the water flowing from the lion-shaped spout retained the characteristic smoothness of Yamaga Onsen.|date=2024-08}} In July 2011 (Heisei 23), redevelopment work on the Plaza Five Building commenced, and in October 2012 (Heisei 24) a restoration of traditional Japanese architecture was completed.{{独自研究範囲|Primarily used by local residents.|date=2024-08}}{{Citation needed範囲|A drinking facility existed in front of the old building’s entrance, providing potable hot spring water 24 hours a day.|date=2024-08}} == History == === Origins === In ancient times, the {{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|lt=Wamyo Ruijusho|ja|倭名類聚抄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wamyo Ruijusho|qq=}}, a dictionary from the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], records the place name "Yun" (温泉) in "Yamaga-gun" (山鹿郡). Yamaga Onsen is considered one of the oldest hot spring resorts in Kumamoto Prefecture, and several legends regarding its origin persist. One theory posits that during the [[:en:Hōgen rebellion|Hōgen Rebellion]], after being defeated and retreating from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Uno Chika-ji|ja|宇野親治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uno Chika-ji|qq=}} was hunting in the mountains and discovered the hot spring upon seeing a herd of {{ill|deer|ja|鹿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/deer|qq=}} lying in a valley. The name "Yamaga" is said to derive from this {{ill|Hot Spring Discovery Legend|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Discovery Legend|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}. Another tradition holds that a descendant of Kikuchi Noritaka’s second son, Masataka—namely, the progenitor of Yamaga Sogyo, {{ill|Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|ja|山鹿太郎重光|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|qq=}}—relocated people to the area in the 3rd year of the [[:en:Jishō|Jishō period]] and constructed bathing facilities, thus laying the foundation for the growth of Yamaga town. === Early Modern Period === During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], as a post town, Yamaga Onsen flourished to the extent that it was said there was "not a single one of a thousand houses missing." At the site where Uno Chika-ji discovered the deer, a bathhouse for the feudal lord was established, and an imperial bath ({{ill|Gomae-yu|ja|御前湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gomae-yu|qq=}}) was installed. === Modern and Contemporary Periods === In 1923 (Taishō 12), the Yamaga Onsen Railway (later known as the {{ill|Yamaga Onsen Railway|ja|山鹿温泉鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Onsen Railway|qq=}}) began operations, with {{ill|Yamaga Station|ja|山鹿駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Station|qq=}} serving as the nearest station; however, it was discontinued in 1960 (Shōwa 35) due to flooding. In November 2006 (Heisei 18), together with three other hot spring areas in the Kikuchi River basin—{{ill|Tama Onsen|ja|玉名温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tama Onsen|qq=}}, {{ill|Kikuchi Onsen|ja|菊池温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Onsen|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ueki Onsen|ja|植木温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueki Onsen|qq=}}—the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort Development Council" was established, aiming to brand the area as the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort" and to develop common products and wide-area tourism routes. == Access == * From the [[:en:Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal|Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal]], take the {{ill|Kyushu Sankō Bus|ja|九州産交バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu Sankō Bus|qq=}} bound for Yamaga Onsen and alight at "Yamaga Onsen (Yachiyo-za Entrance)." * Approximately 12 km from the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]] {{ill|Ueki Interchange|ja|植木インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueki Interchange|qq=}}, and 9.5 km from the {{ill|Kikusui Interchange|ja|菊水インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikusui Interchange|qq=}}. == Related Topics == * Hot springs in Yamaga City ** {{ill|Hirayama Onsen|ja|平山温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirayama Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Kumairi Onsen|ja|熊入温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumairi Onsen|qq=}} == External Links == Association|ja|山鹿温泉観光協会}]] * [https://yamaga-tanbou.jp/genre/onsen/ {{ill|Yamaga Onsen|ja|山鹿温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Onsen|qq=}}] – Yamaga City {{Onsen}} {{Onsen-stub}} {{混同|Yamakawa Onsen}} {{infobox Onsen | Name = Yamaga Onsen (山鹿温泉) | image = [[File:Sakura-yu Yamaga ac (1).jpg|280px]]
Communal bath {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura-yu|qq=}} | Comment = | Location = [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]][[:en:Yamaga, Kumamoto|Yamaga City]] {{Japanese Location Information|33|0|53.1|130|41|20.3|Yamaga Onsen|33.01475,130.688972|Yamaga Onsen|nocoord=yes}} {{Location map|Japan Kumamoto Prefecture|relief=1|float=center|width=225}} | Latitude Degrees = 33 | Latitude Minutes = 0 | Latitude Seconds = 53.1 | Longitude Degrees = 130 | Longitude Minutes = 41 | Longitude Seconds = 20.3 | Access = ''For details, see the [[#Access|Access]] section.'' | Spring Quality = {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} | Spring Temperature = | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = Alkaline | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = [https://yamaga-tanbou.jp/genre/onsen/ Official site of Yamaga Onsen in Yamaga City] | Special Notes = }} '''Yamaga Onsen''' (やまがおんせん) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]][[:en:Yamaga, Kumamoto|Yamaga City]] (formerly part of [[:en:Higo Province|Higo Province]]). It is renowned for the annual "Yamaga Lantern Festival" ({{ill|Yamaga Tōrō Matsuri|ja|山鹿灯籠まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Tōrō Matsuri|qq=}}) held every August. A communal bath (public bath) known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura-yu|qq=}} is available. == Spring Quality == * Alkaline simple hot spring It is reputed for its exceptionally smooth water that is said to have excellent beautifying effects. == Hot Spring Town == [[File:yamaga_ashiyu.jpg|thumb|"Ashiyu"]] There are approximately 20 establishments including [[:en:Ryokan|ryokan]] and [[:en:Hotel|hotels]], making it one of the largest hot spring towns in Kumamoto Prefecture. As an old hot spring town, it emphasizes its historic charm, and it is said that during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] it was modeled after [[:en:Dōgo Onsen|Dogo Onsen]]. Although it is located within the urban area, creatively designed open-air baths and bathing rooms enhance its quaint atmosphere. In addition to "Ashiyu" and communal baths, a hot spring pool (now no longer extant) was once established. Along the former {{ill|Buzen Highway|ja|豊前街道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buzen Highway|qq=}}, there stands the Yachiyo-za, a theater building completed in 1910 (Meiji 45). === Communal Bath === A communal bath known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura-yu|qq=}} exists. It was originally constructed by inviting the master carpenter who built the main building of {{ill|Dogo Onsen Honkan|ja|道後温泉本館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dogo Onsen Honkan|qq=}}. revision.comment: Bot: update category → Hot springs of Kumamoto prefecture
revision.text:
{{混同|Yamakawa Onsen}} {{infobox Onsen | Name = Yamaga Onsen (山鹿温泉) | image = [[File:Sakura-yu Yamaga ac (1).jpg|280px]]
Communal bath {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura-yu|qq=}} | Comment = | Location = [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]][[:en:Yamaga, Kumamoto|Yamaga City]] {{Japanese Location Information|33|0|53.1|130|41|20.3|Yamaga Onsen|33.01475,130.688972|Yamaga Onsen|nocoord=yes}} {{Location map|Japan Kumamoto Prefecture|relief=1|float=center|width=225}} | Latitude Degrees = 33 | Latitude Minutes = 0 | Latitude Seconds = 53.1 | Longitude Degrees = 130 | Longitude Minutes = 41 | Longitude Seconds = 20.3 | Access = ''For details, see the [[#Access|Access]] section.'' | Spring Quality = {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} | Spring Temperature = | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = Alkaline | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = [https://yamaga-tanbou.jp/genre/onsen/ Official site of Yamaga Onsen in Yamaga City] | Special Notes = }} '''Yamaga Onsen''' (やまがおんせん) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]][[:en:Yamaga, Kumamoto|Yamaga City]] (formerly part of [[:en:Higo Province|Higo Province]]). It is renowned for the annual "Yamaga Lantern Festival" ({{ill|Yamaga Tōrō Matsuri|ja|山鹿灯籠まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Tōrō Matsuri|qq=}}) held every August. A communal bath (public bath) known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura-yu|qq=}} is available. == Spring Quality == * Alkaline simple hot spring It is reputed for its exceptionally smooth water that is said to have excellent beautifying effects. == Hot Spring Town == [[File:yamaga_ashiyu.jpg|thumb|"Ashiyu"]] There are approximately 20 establishments including [[:en:Ryokan|ryokan]] and [[:en:Hotel|hotels]], making it one of the largest hot spring towns in Kumamoto Prefecture. As an old hot spring town, it emphasizes its historic charm, and it is said that during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] it was modeled after [[:en:Dōgo Onsen|Dogo Onsen]]. Although it is located within the urban area, creatively designed open-air baths and bathing rooms enhance its quaint atmosphere. In addition to "Ashiyu" and communal baths, a hot spring pool (now no longer extant) was once established. Along the former {{ill|Buzen Highway|ja|豊前街道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buzen Highway|qq=}}, there stands the Yachiyo-za, a theater building completed in 1910 (Meiji 45). === Communal Bath === A communal bath known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura-yu|qq=}} exists. It was originally constructed by inviting the master carpenter who built the main building of {{ill|Dogo Onsen Honkan|ja|道後温泉本館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dogo Onsen Honkan|qq=}}. In a redevelopment in 1973 (Shōwa 48), it was incorporated into the Plaza Five Building, preserving only the original eave ([[:en:Bargeboard|hafu]]).{{Citation needed範囲|The interior was modern and at first resembled a pool, yet the water flowing from the lion-shaped spout retained the characteristic smoothness of Yamaga Onsen.|date=2024-08}} In July 2011 (Heisei 23), redevelopment work on the Plaza Five Building commenced, and in October 2012 (Heisei 24) a restoration of traditional Japanese architecture was completed.{{独自研究範囲|Primarily used by local residents.|date=2024-08}}{{Citation needed範囲|A drinking facility existed in front of the old building’s entrance, providing potable hot spring water 24 hours a day.|date=2024-08}} == History == === Origins === In ancient times, the {{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|lt=Wamyo Ruijusho|ja|倭名類聚抄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wamyo Ruijusho|qq=}}, a dictionary from the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], records the place name "Yun" (温泉) in "Yamaga-gun" (山鹿郡). Yamaga Onsen is considered one of the oldest hot spring resorts in Kumamoto Prefecture, and several legends regarding its origin persist. One theory posits that during the [[:en:Hōgen rebellion|Hōgen Rebellion]], after being defeated and retreating from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Uno Chika-ji|ja|宇野親治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uno Chika-ji|qq=}} was hunting in the mountains and discovered the hot spring upon seeing a herd of {{ill|deer|ja|鹿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/deer|qq=}} lying in a valley. The name "Yamaga" is said to derive from this {{ill|Hot Spring Discovery Legend|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Discovery Legend|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}. Another tradition holds that a descendant of Kikuchi Noritaka’s second son, Masataka—namely, the progenitor of Yamaga Sogyo, {{ill|Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|ja|山鹿太郎重光|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|qq=}}—relocated people to the area in the 3rd year of the [[:en:Jishō|Jishō period]] and constructed bathing facilities, thus laying the foundation for the growth of Yamaga town. === Early Modern Period === During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], as a post town, Yamaga Onsen flourished to the extent that it was said there was "not a single one of a thousand houses missing." At the site where Uno Chika-ji discovered the deer, a bathhouse for the feudal lord was established, and an imperial bath ({{ill|Gomae-yu|ja|御前湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gomae-yu|qq=}}) was installed. === Modern and Contemporary Periods === In 1923 (Taishō 12), the Yamaga Onsen Railway (later known as the {{ill|Yamaga Onsen Railway|ja|山鹿温泉鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Onsen Railway|qq=}}) began operations, with {{ill|Yamaga Station|ja|山鹿駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Station|qq=}} serving as the nearest station; however, it was discontinued in 1960 (Shōwa 35) due to flooding. In November 2006 (Heisei 18), together with three other hot spring areas in the Kikuchi River basin—{{ill|Tama Onsen|ja|玉名温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tama Onsen|qq=}}, {{ill|Kikuchi Onsen|ja|菊池温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Onsen|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ueki Onsen|ja|植木温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueki Onsen|qq=}}—the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort Development Council" was established, aiming to brand the area as the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort" and to develop common products and wide-area tourism routes. == Access == * From the [[:en:Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal|Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal]], take the {{ill|Kyushu Sankō Bus|ja|九州産交バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu Sankō Bus|qq=}} bound for Yamaga Onsen and alight at "Yamaga Onsen (Yachiyo-za Entrance)." * Approximately 12 km from the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]] {{ill|Ueki Interchange|ja|植木インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueki Interchange|qq=}}, and 9.5 km from the {{ill|Kikusui Interchange|ja|菊水インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikusui Interchange|qq=}}. == Related Topics == * Hot springs in Yamaga City ** {{ill|Hirayama Onsen|ja|平山温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirayama Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Kumairi Onsen|ja|熊入温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumairi Onsen|qq=}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Yamaga Onsen}} {{Wiki Portal Link|Hot Springs|[[File:Hot springs 001.svg|34px|Portal: Hot Springs]]}} * [https://www.y-kankoukyoukai.com/ {{ill|Yamaga Onsen Tourism Association|ja|山鹿温泉観光協会}]] * [https://yamaga-tanbou.jp/genre/onsen/ {{ill|Yamaga Onsen|ja|山鹿温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Onsen|qq=}}] – Yamaga City {{Onsen}} {{Onsen-stub}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|山鹿温泉|version=104200222|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] [[Category:Hot Springs in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E9%B9%BF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89]] [[sn:Yamaga Onsen]] [[en:Yamaga Onsen]] [[zh:%E5%B1%B1%E9%B9%BF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89]] [[Category:Yamaga, Kumamoto]] [[Category:Hot springs of Kumamoto prefecture]]

Along the former {{ill|Buzen Highway|ja|豊前街道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buzen Highway|qq=}}, there stands the Yachiyo-za, a theater building completed in 1910 (Meiji 45). === Communal Bath === A communal bath known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura-yu|qq=}} exists. It was originally constructed by inviting the master carpenter who built the main building of {{ill|Dogo Onsen Honkan|ja|道後温泉本館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dogo Onsen Honkan|qq=}}. In a redevelopment in 1973 (Shōwa 48), it was incorporated into the Plaza Five Building, preserving only the original eave ([[:en:Bargeboard|hafu]]).{{Citation needed範囲|The interior was modern and at first resembled a pool, yet the water flowing from the lion-shaped spout retained the characteristic smoothness of Yamaga Onsen.|date=2024-08}} In July 2011 (Heisei 23), redevelopment work on the Plaza Five Building commenced, and in October 2012 (Heisei 24) a restoration of traditional Japanese architecture was completed.{{独自研究範囲|Primarily used by local residents.|date=2024-08}}{{Citation needed範囲|A drinking facility existed in front of the old building’s entrance, providing potable hot spring water 24 hours a day.|date=2024-08}} == History == === Origins === In ancient times, the {{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|lt=Wamyo Ruijusho|ja|倭名類聚抄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wamyo Ruijusho|qq=}}, a dictionary from the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], records the place name "Yun" (温泉) in "Yamaga-gun" (山鹿郡). Yamaga Onsen is considered one of the oldest hot spring resorts in Kumamoto Prefecture, and several legends regarding its origin persist. One theory posits that during the [[:en:Hōgen rebellion|Hōgen Rebellion]], after being defeated and retreating from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Uno Chika-ji|ja|宇野親治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uno Chika-ji|qq=}} was hunting in the mountains and discovered the hot spring upon seeing a herd of {{ill|deer|ja|鹿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/deer|qq=}} lying in a valley. The name "Yamaga" is said to derive from this {{ill|Hot Spring Discovery Legend|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Discovery Legend|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}. Another tradition holds that a descendant of Kikuchi Noritaka’s second son, Masataka—namely, the progenitor of Yamaga Sogyo, {{ill|Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|ja|山鹿太郎重光|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|qq=}}—relocated people to the area in the 3rd year of the [[:en:Jishō|Jishō period]] and constructed bathing facilities, thus laying the foundation for the growth of Yamaga town. === Early Modern Period === During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], as a post town, Yamaga Onsen flourished to the extent that it was said there was "not a single one of a thousand houses missing." At the site where Uno Chika-ji discovered the deer, a bathhouse for the feudal lord was established, and an imperial bath ({{ill|Gomae-yu|ja|御前湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gomae-yu|qq=}}) was installed. === Modern and Contemporary Periods === In 1923 (Taishō 12), the Yamaga Onsen Railway (later known as the {{ill|Yamaga Onsen Railway|ja|山鹿温泉鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Onsen Railway|qq=}}) began operations, with {{ill|Yamaga Station|ja|山鹿駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Station|qq=}} serving as the nearest station; however, it was discontinued in 1960 (Shōwa 35) due to flooding. In November 2006 (Heisei 18), together with three other hot spring areas in the Kikuchi River basin—{{ill|Tama Onsen|ja|玉名温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tama Onsen|qq=}}, {{ill|Kikuchi Onsen|ja|菊池温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Onsen|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ueki Onsen|ja|植木温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueki Onsen|qq=}}—the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort Development Council" was established, aiming to brand the area as the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort" and to develop common products and wide-area tourism routes. == Access == * From the [[:en:Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal|Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal]], take the {{ill|Kyushu Sankō Bus|ja|九州産交バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu Sankō Bus|qq=}} bound for Yamaga Onsen and alight at "Yamaga Onsen (Yachiyo-za Entrance)." * Approximately 12 km from the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]] {{ill|Ueki Interchange|ja|植木インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueki Interchange|qq=}}, and 9.5 km from the {{ill|Kikusui Interchange|ja|菊水インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikusui Interchange|qq=}}. == Related Topics == * Hot springs in Yamaga City ** {{ill|Hirayama Onsen|ja|平山温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirayama Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Kumairi Onsen|ja|熊入温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumairi Onsen|qq=}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Yamaga Onsen}} {{Wiki Portal Link|Hot Springs|[[File:Hot springs 001.svg|34px|Portal: Hot Springs]]}} * [https://www.y-kankoukyoukai.com/ {{ill|Yamaga Onsen Tourism Association|ja|山鹿温泉観光協会}]] * [https://yamaga-tanbou.jp/genre/onsen/ {{ill|Yamaga Onsen|ja|山鹿温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Onsen|qq=}}] – Yamaga City {{Onsen}} {{Onsen-stub}} Kumamoto]] [[Category:Hot springs of Kumamoto prefecture]] {{Commons category|Yamaga Onsen}} [[ja:山鹿温泉]] [[zh:山鹿温泉]] {{Commons category|Yamaga Onsen}} [[ja:山鹿温泉]] [[zh:山鹿温泉]]

{{混同|Yamakawa Onsen}} {{infobox Onsen | Name = Yamaga Onsen (山鹿温泉) | image = [[File:Sakura-yu Yamaga ac (1).jpg|280px]]
Communal bath {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura-yu|qq=}} | Comment = | Location = [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]][[:en:Yamaga, Kumamoto|Yamaga City]] {{Japanese Location Information|33|0|53.1|130|41|20.3|Yamaga Onsen|33.01475,130.688972|Yamaga Onsen|nocoord=yes}} {{Location map|Japan Kumamoto Prefecture|relief=1|float=center|width=225}} | Latitude Degrees = 33 | Latitude Minutes = 0 | Latitude Seconds = 53.1 | Longitude Degrees = 130 | Longitude Minutes = 41 | Longitude Seconds = 20.3 | Access = ''For details, see the [[#Access|Access]] section.'' | Spring Quality = {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} | Spring Temperature = | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = Alkaline | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = [https://yamaga-tanbou.jp/genre/onsen/ Official site of Yamaga Onsen in Yamaga City] | Special Notes = }} '''Yamaga Onsen''' (やまがおんせん) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]][[:en:Yamaga, Kumamoto|Yamaga City]] (formerly part of [[:en:Higo Province|Higo Province]]). It is renowned for the annual "Yamaga Lantern Festival" ({{ill|Yamaga Tōrō Matsuri|ja|山鹿灯籠まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Tōrō Matsuri|qq=}}) held every August. A communal bath (public bath) known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura-yu|qq=}} is available. == Spring Quality == * Alkaline simple hot spring It is reputed for its exceptionally smooth water that is said to have excellent beautifying effects. == Hot Spring Town == [[File:yamaga_ashiyu.jpg|thumb|"Ashiyu"]] There are approximately 20 establishments including [[:en:Ryokan|ryokan]] and [[:en:Hotel|hotels]], making it one of the largest hot spring towns in Kumamoto Prefecture. As an old hot spring town, it emphasizes its historic charm, and it is said that during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] it was modeled after [[:en:Dōgo Onsen|Dogo Onsen]]. Although it is located within the urban area, creatively designed open-air baths and bathing rooms enhance its quaint atmosphere. In addition to "Ashiyu" and communal baths, a hot spring pool (now no longer extant) was once established. Along the former {{ill|Buzen Highway|ja|豊前街道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buzen Highway|qq=}}, there stands the Yachiyo-za, a theater building completed in 1910 (Meiji 45). === Communal Bath === A communal bath known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura-yu|qq=}} exists. It was originally constructed by inviting the master carpenter who built the main building of {{ill|Dogo Onsen Honkan|ja|道後温泉本館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dogo Onsen Honkan|qq=}}. In a redevelopment in 1973 (Shōwa 48), it was incorporated into the Plaza Five Building, preserving only the original eave ([[:en:Bargeboard|hafu]]).{{Citation needed範囲|The interior was modern and at first resembled a pool, yet the water flowing from the lion-shaped spout retained the characteristic smoothness of Yamaga Onsen.|date=2024-08}} In July 2011 (Heisei 23), redevelopment work on the Plaza Five Building commenced, and in October 2012 (Heisei 24) a restoration of traditional Japanese architecture was completed.{{独自研究範囲|Primarily used by local residents.|date=2024-08}}{{Citation needed範囲|A drinking facility existed in front of the old building’s entrance, providing potable hot spring water 24 hours a day.|date=2024-08}} == History == === Origins === In ancient times, the {{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|lt=Wamyo Ruijusho|ja|倭名類聚抄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wamyo Ruijusho|qq=}}, a dictionary from the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], records the place name "Yun" (温泉) in "Yamaga-gun" (山鹿郡). Yamaga Onsen is considered one of the oldest hot spring resorts in Kumamoto Prefecture, and several legends regarding its origin persist. One theory posits that during the [[:en:Hōgen rebellion|Hōgen Rebellion]], after being defeated and retreating from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Uno Chika-ji|ja|宇野親治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uno Chika-ji|qq=}} was hunting in the mountains and discovered the hot spring upon seeing a herd of {{ill|deer|ja|鹿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/deer|qq=}} lying in a valley. The name "Yamaga" is said to derive from this {{ill|Hot Spring Discovery Legend|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Discovery Legend|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}. Another tradition holds that a descendant of Kikuchi Noritaka’s second son, Masataka—namely, the progenitor of Yamaga Sogyo, {{ill|Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|ja|山鹿太郎重光|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|qq=}}—relocated people to the area in the 3rd year of the [[:en:Jishō|Jishō period]] and constructed bathing facilities, thus laying the foundation for the growth of Yamaga town. === Early Modern Period === During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], as a post town, Yamaga Onsen flourished to the extent that it was said there was "not a single one of a thousand houses missing." At the site where Uno Chika-ji discovered the deer, a bathhouse for the feudal lord was established, and an imperial bath ({{ill|Gomae-yu|ja|御前湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gomae-yu|qq=}}) was installed. === Modern and Contemporary Periods === In 1923 (Taishō 12), the Yamaga Onsen Railway (later known as the {{ill|Yamaga Onsen Railway|ja|山鹿温泉鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Onsen Railway|qq=}}) began operations, with {{ill|Yamaga Station|ja|山鹿駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Station|qq=}} serving as the nearest station; however, it was discontinued in 1960 (Shōwa 35) due to flooding. In November 2006 (Heisei 18), together with three other hot spring areas in the Kikuchi River basin—{{ill|Tama Onsen|ja|玉名温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tama Onsen|qq=}}, {{ill|Kikuchi Onsen|ja|菊池温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Onsen|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ueki Onsen|ja|植木温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueki Onsen|qq=}}—the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort Development Council" was established, aiming to brand the area as the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort" and to develop common products and wide-area tourism routes. == Access == * From the [[:en:Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal|Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal]], take the {{ill|Kyushu Sankō Bus|ja|九州産交バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu Sankō Bus|qq=}} bound for Yamaga Onsen and alight at "Yamaga Onsen (Yachiyo-za Entrance)." * Approximately 12 km from the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]] {{ill|Ueki Interchange|ja|植木インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueki Interchange|qq=}}, and 9.5 km from the {{ill|Kikusui Interchange|ja|菊水インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikusui Interchange|qq=}}. == Related Topics == * Hot springs in Yamaga City ** {{ill|Hirayama Interchange|ja|菊水インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikusui Interchange|qq=}}. == Related Topics == * Hot springs in Yamaga City ** {{ill|Hirayama Onsen|ja|平山温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirayama Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Kumairi Onsen|ja|熊入温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumairi Onsen|qq=}} == External Links == revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Hot Springs in Kumamoto Prefecture
revision.text:
{{混同|Yamakawa Onsen}} {{infobox Onsen | Name = Yamaga Onsen (山鹿温泉) | image = [[File:Sakura-yu Yamaga ac (1).jpg|280px]]
Communal bath {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura-yu|qq=}} | Comment = | Location = [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]][[:en:Yamaga, Kumamoto|Yamaga City]] {{Japanese Location Information|33|0|53.1|130|41|20.3|Yamaga Onsen|33.01475,130.688972|Yamaga Onsen|nocoord=yes}} {{Location map|Japan Kumamoto Prefecture|relief=1|float=center|width=225}} | Latitude Degrees = 33 | Latitude Minutes = 0 | Latitude Seconds = 53.1 | Longitude Degrees = 130 | Longitude Minutes = 41 | Longitude Seconds = 20.3 | Access = ''For details, see the [[#Access|Access]] section.'' | Spring Quality = {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} | Spring Temperature = | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = Alkaline | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = [https://yamaga-tanbou.jp/genre/onsen/ Official site of Yamaga Onsen in Yamaga City] | Special Notes = }} '''Yamaga Onsen''' (やまがおんせん) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]][[:en:Yamaga, Kumamoto|Yamaga City]] (formerly part of [[:en:Higo Province|Higo Province]]). It is renowned for the annual "Yamaga Lantern Festival" ({{ill|Yamaga Tōrō Matsuri|ja|山鹿灯籠まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Tōrō Matsuri|qq=}}) held every August. A communal bath (public bath) known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura-yu|qq=}} is available. == Spring Quality == * Alkaline simple hot spring It is reputed for its exceptionally smooth water that is said to have excellent beautifying effects. == Hot Spring Town == [[File:yamaga_ashiyu.jpg|thumb|"Ashiyu"]] There are approximately 20 establishments including [[:en:Ryokan|ryokan]] and [[:en:Hotel|hotels]], making it one of the largest hot spring towns in Kumamoto Prefecture. As an old hot spring town, it emphasizes its historic charm, and it is said that during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] it was modeled after [[:en:Dōgo Onsen|Dogo Onsen]]. Although it is located within the urban area, creatively designed open-air baths and bathing rooms enhance its quaint atmosphere. In addition to "Ashiyu" and communal baths, a hot spring pool (now no longer extant) was once established. Along the former {{ill|Buzen Highway|ja|豊前街道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buzen Highway|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, there stands the Yachiyo-za, a theater building completed in 1910 (Meiji 45). === Communal Bath === A communal bath known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura-yu|qq=}} exists. It was originally constructed by inviting the master carpenter who built the main building of {{ill|Dogo Onsen Honkan|ja|道後温泉本館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dogo Onsen Honkan|qq=}}. In a redevelopment in 1973 (Shōwa 48), it was incorporated into the Plaza Five Building, preserving only the original eave ([[:en:Bargeboard|hafu]]).{{Citation needed範囲|The interior was modern and at first resembled a pool, yet the water flowing from the lion-shaped spout retained the characteristic smoothness of Yamaga Onsen.|date=2024-08}} In July 2011 (Heisei 23), redevelopment work on the Plaza Five Building commenced, and in October 2012 (Heisei 24) a restoration of traditional Japanese architecture was completed.{{独自研究範囲|Primarily used by local residents.|date=2024-08}}{{Citation needed範囲|A drinking facility existed in front of the old building’s entrance, providing potable hot spring water 24 hours a day.|date=2024-08}} == History == === Origins === In ancient times, the {{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|lt=Wamyo Ruijusho|ja|倭名類聚抄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wamyo Ruijusho|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和名類聚抄}}, a dictionary from the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], records the place name "Yun" (温泉) in "Yamaga-gun" (山鹿郡). Yamaga Onsen is considered one of the oldest hot spring resorts in Kumamoto Prefecture, and several legends regarding its origin persist. One theory posits that during the [[:en:Hōgen rebellion|Hōgen Rebellion]], after being defeated and retreating from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Uno Chika-ji|ja|宇野親治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uno Chika-ji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 源親治}} was hunting in the mountains and discovered the hot spring upon seeing a herd of {{ill|deer|ja|鹿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/deer|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シカ}} lying in a valley. The name "Yamaga" is said to derive from this {{ill|Hot Spring Discovery Legend|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Discovery Legend|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}. Another tradition holds that a descendant of Kikuchi Noritaka’s second son, Masataka—namely, the progenitor of Yamaga Sogyo, {{ill|Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|ja|山鹿太郎重光|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—relocated people to the area in the 3rd year of the [[:en:Jishō|Jishō period]] and constructed bathing facilities, thus laying the foundation for the growth of Yamaga town. === Early Modern Period === During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], as a post town, Yamaga Onsen flourished to the extent that it was said there was "not a single one of a thousand houses missing." At the site where Uno Chika-ji discovered the deer, a bathhouse for the feudal lord was established, and an imperial bath ({{ill|Gomae-yu|ja|御前湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gomae-yu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was installed. === Modern and Contemporary Periods === In 1923 (Taishō 12), the Yamaga Onsen Railway (later known as the {{ill|Yamaga Onsen Railway|ja|山鹿温泉鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Onsen Railway|qq=}}) began operations, with {{ill|Yamaga Station|ja|山鹿駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Station|qq=}} serving as the nearest station; however, it was discontinued in 1960 (Shōwa 35) due to flooding. In November 2006 (Heisei 18), together with three other hot spring areas in the Kikuchi River basin—{{ill|Tama Onsen|ja|玉名温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tama Onsen|qq=}}, {{ill|Kikuchi Onsen|ja|菊池温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Onsen|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ueki Onsen|ja|植木温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueki Onsen|qq=}}—the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort Development Council" was established, aiming to brand the area as the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort" and to develop common products and wide-area tourism routes. == Access == * From the [[:en:Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal|Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal]], take the {{ill|Kyushu Sankō Bus|ja|九州産交バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu Sankō Bus|qq=}} bound for Yamaga Onsen and alight at "Yamaga Onsen (Yachiyo-za Entrance)." * Approximately 12 km from the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]] {{ill|Ueki Interchange|ja|植木インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueki Interchange|qq=}}, and 9.5 km from the {{ill|Kikusui Interchange|ja|菊水インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikusui Interchange|qq=}}. == Related Topics == * Hot springs in Yamaga City ** {{ill|Hirayama Onsen|ja|平山温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirayama Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Kumairi Onsen|ja|熊入温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumairi Onsen|qq=}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Yamaga Onsen}} {{Wiki Portal Link|Hot Springs|[[File:Hot springs 001.svg|34px|Portal: Hot Springs]]}} * [https://www.y-kankoukyoukai.com/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Yamaga Onsen Tourism Association|ja|山鹿温泉観光協会}]] * [https://yamaga-tanbou.jp/genre/onsen/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Yamaga Onsen|ja|山鹿温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Onsen|qq=}}] – Yamaga City {{Onsen}} {{Onsen-stub}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|山鹿温泉|version=104200222|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E9%B9%BF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89]] [[sn:Yamaga Onsen]] [[zh:%E5%B1%B1%E9%B9%BF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89]] [[Category:Yamaga, Kumamoto]] [[Category:Hot springs of Kumamoto prefecture]] {{Commons category|Yamaga Onsen}} [[ja:山鹿温泉]] [[zh:山鹿温泉]] {{Commons category|Yamaga Onsen}} [[ja:山鹿温泉]] [[zh:山鹿温泉]]

As an old hot spring town, it emphasizes its historic charm, and it is said that during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] it was modeled after [[:en:Dōgo Onsen|Dogo Onsen]]. Although it is located within the urban area, creatively designed open-air baths and bathing rooms enhance its quaint atmosphere. In addition to "Ashiyu" and communal baths, a hot spring pool (now no longer extant) was once established. Along the former {{ill|Buzen Highway|ja|豊前街道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buzen Highway|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, there stands the Yachiyo-za, a theater building completed in 1910 (Meiji 45). === Communal Bath === A communal bath known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura-yu|qq=}} exists. It was originally constructed by inviting the master carpenter who built the main building of {{ill|Dogo Onsen Honkan|ja|道後温泉本館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dogo Onsen Honkan|qq=}}. In a redevelopment in 1973 (Shōwa 48), it was incorporated into the Plaza Five Building, preserving only the original eave ([[:en:Bargeboard|hafu]]).{{Citation needed範囲|The interior was modern and at first resembled a pool, yet the water flowing from the lion-shaped spout retained the characteristic smoothness of Yamaga Onsen.|date=2024-08}} In July 2011 (Heisei 23), redevelopment work on the Plaza Five Building commenced, and in October 2012 (Heisei 24) a restoration of traditional Japanese architecture was completed.{{独自研究範囲|Primarily used by local residents.|date=2024-08}}{{Citation needed範囲|A drinking facility existed in front of the old building’s entrance, providing potable hot spring water 24 hours a day.|date=2024-08}} == History == === Origins === In ancient times, the {{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|lt=Wamyo Ruijusho|ja|倭名類聚抄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wamyo Ruijusho|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=和名類聚抄}}, a dictionary from the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], records the place name "Yun" (温泉) in "Yamaga-gun" (山鹿郡). Yamaga Onsen is considered one of the oldest hot spring resorts in Kumamoto Prefecture, and several legends regarding its origin persist. One theory posits that during the [[:en:Hōgen rebellion|Hōgen Rebellion]], after being defeated and retreating from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Uno Chika-ji|ja|宇野親治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uno Chika-ji|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=源親治}} was hunting in the mountains and discovered the hot spring upon seeing a herd of {{ill|deer|ja|鹿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/deer|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=シカ}} lying in a valley. The name "Yamaga" is said to derive from this {{ill|Hot Spring Discovery Legend|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Discovery Legend|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}. Another tradition holds that a descendant of Kikuchi Noritaka’s second son, Masataka—namely, the progenitor of Yamaga Sogyo, {{ill|Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|ja|山鹿太郎重光|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—relocated people to the area in the 3rd year of the [[:en:Jishō|Jishō period]] and constructed bathing facilities, thus laying the foundation for the growth of Yamaga town. === Early Modern Period === During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], as a post town, Yamaga Onsen flourished to the extent that it was said there was "not a single one of a thousand houses missing." At the site where Uno Chika-ji discovered the deer, a bathhouse for the feudal lord was established, and an imperial bath ({{ill|Gomae-yu|ja|御前湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gomae-yu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was installed. === Modern and Contemporary Periods === In 1923 (Taishō 12), the Yamaga Onsen Railway (later known as the {{ill|Yamaga Onsen Railway|ja|山鹿温泉鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Onsen Railway|qq=}}) began operations, with {{ill|Yamaga Station|ja|山鹿駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Station|qq=}} serving as the nearest station; however, it was discontinued in 1960 (Shōwa 35) due to flooding. In November 2006 (Heisei 18), together with three other hot spring areas in the Kikuchi River basin—{{ill|Tama Onsen|ja|玉名温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tama Onsen|qq=}}, {{ill|Kikuchi Onsen|ja|菊池温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Onsen|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ueki Onsen|ja|植木温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueki Onsen|qq=}}—the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort Development Council" was established, aiming to brand the area as the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort" and to develop common products and wide-area tourism routes. == Access == * From the [[:en:Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal|Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal]], take the {{ill|Kyushu Sankō Bus|ja|九州産交バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu Sankō Bus|qq=}} bound for Yamaga Onsen and alight at "Yamaga Onsen (Yachiyo-za Entrance)." * Approximately 12 km from the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]] {{ill|Ueki Interchange|ja|植木インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueki Interchange|qq=}}, and 9.5 km from the {{ill|Kikusui Interchange|ja|菊水インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikusui Interchange|qq=}}. == Related Topics == * Hot springs in Yamaga City ** {{ill|Hirayama Onsen|ja|平山温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirayama Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Kumairi Onsen|ja|熊入温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumairi Onsen|qq=}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Yamaga Onsen}} {{Wiki Portal Link|Hot Springs|[[File:Hot springs 001.svg|34px|Portal: Hot Springs]]}} * [https://www.y-kankoukyoukai.com/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Yamaga Onsen Tourism Association|ja|山鹿温泉観光協会}]] * [https://yamaga-tanbou.jp/genre/onsen/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Yamaga Onsen|ja|山鹿温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Onsen|qq=}}] – Yamaga City {{Onsen}} {{Onsen-stub}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|山鹿温泉|version=104200222|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E9%B9%BF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89]] [[sn:Yamaga Onsen]] [[zh:%E5%B1%B1%E9%B9%BF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89]] [[Category:Yamaga, Kumamoto]] [[Category:Hot springs of Kumamoto prefecture]] {{Commons category|Yamaga Onsen}} [[ja:山鹿温泉]] [[zh:山鹿温泉]] {{Commons category|Yamaga Onsen}} [[ja:山鹿温泉]] [[zh:山鹿温泉]]

It is renowned for the annual "Yamaga Lantern Festival" ({{ill|Yamaga Tōrō Matsuri|ja|山鹿灯籠まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Tōrō Matsuri|qq=}}) held every August. A communal bath (public bath) known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura-yu|qq=}} is available. == Spring Quality == * Alkaline simple hot spring It is reputed for its exceptionally smooth water that is said to have excellent beautifying effects. == Hot Spring Town == [[File:yamaga_ashiyu.jpg|thumb|"Ashiyu"]] There are approximately 20 establishments including [[:en:Ryokan|ryokan]] and [[:en:Hotel|hotels]], making it one of the largest hot spring towns in Kumamoto Prefecture. As an old hot spring town, it emphasizes its historic charm, and it is said that during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] it was modeled after [[:en:Dōgo Onsen|Dogo Onsen]]. Although it is located within the urban area, creatively designed open-air baths and bathing rooms enhance its quaint atmosphere. In addition to "Ashiyu" and communal baths, a hot spring pool (now no longer extant) was once established. Along the former {{ill|Buzen Highway|ja|豊前街道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buzen Highway|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, there stands the Yachiyo-za, a theater building completed in 1910 (Meiji 45). === Communal Bath === A communal bath known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura-yu|qq=}} exists. It was originally constructed by inviting the master carpenter who built the main building of {{ill|Dogo Onsen Honkan|ja|道後温泉本館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dogo Onsen Honkan|qq=}}. In a redevelopment in 1973 (Shōwa 48), it was incorporated into the Plaza Five Building, preserving only the original eave ([[:en:Bargeboard|hafu]]).{{Citation needed範囲|The interior was modern and at first resembled a pool, yet the water flowing from the lion-shaped spout retained the characteristic smoothness of Yamaga Onsen.|date=2024-08}} In July 2011 (Heisei 23), redevelopment work on the Plaza Five Building commenced, and in October 2012 (Heisei 24) a restoration of traditional Japanese architecture was completed.{{独自研究範囲|Primarily used by local residents.|date=2024-08}}{{Citation needed範囲|A drinking facility existed in front of the old building’s entrance, providing potable hot spring water 24 hours a day.|date=2024-08}} == History == === Origins === In ancient times, the {{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|lt=Wamyo Ruijusho|ja|倭名類聚抄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wamyo Ruijusho|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=和名類聚抄}}, a dictionary from the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], records the place name "Yun" (温泉) in "Yamaga-gun" (山鹿郡). Yamaga Onsen is considered one of the oldest hot spring resorts in Kumamoto Prefecture, and several legends regarding its origin persist. One theory posits that during the [[:en:Hōgen rebellion|Hōgen Rebellion]], after being defeated and retreating from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Uno Chika-ji|ja|宇野親治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uno Chika-ji|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=源親治}} was hunting in the mountains and discovered the hot spring upon seeing a herd of {{ill|deer|ja|鹿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/deer|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=シカ}} lying in a valley. The name "Yamaga" is said to derive from this {{ill|Hot Spring Discovery Legend|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Discovery Legend|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}. Another tradition holds that a descendant of Kikuchi Noritaka’s second son, Masataka—namely, the progenitor of Yamaga Sogyo, {{ill|Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|ja|山鹿太郎重光|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—relocated people to the area in the 3rd year of the [[:en:Jishō|Jishō period]] and constructed bathing facilities, thus laying the foundation for the growth of Yamaga town. === Early Modern Period === During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], as a post town, Yamaga Onsen flourished to the extent that it was said there was "not a single one of a thousand houses missing." At the site where Uno Chika-ji discovered the deer, a bathhouse for the feudal lord was established, and an imperial bath ({{ill|Gomae-yu|ja|御前湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gomae-yu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was installed. === Modern and Contemporary Periods === In 1923 (Taishō 12), the Yamaga Onsen Railway (later known as the {{ill|Yamaga Onsen Railway|ja|山鹿温泉鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Onsen Railway|qq=}}) began operations, with {{ill|Yamaga Station|ja|山鹿駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Station|qq=}} serving as the nearest station; however, it was discontinued in 1960 (Shōwa 35) due to flooding. In November 2006 (Heisei 18), together with three other hot spring areas in the Kikuchi River basin—{{ill|Tama Onsen|ja|玉名温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tama Onsen|qq=}}, {{ill|Kikuchi Onsen|ja|菊池温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Onsen|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ueki Onsen|ja|植木温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueki Onsen|qq=}}—the bound for Yamaga Onsen and alight at "Yamaga Onsen (Yachiyo-za Entrance)." * Approximately 12 km from the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]] {{ill|Ueki At the site where Uno Chika-ji discovered the deer, a bathhouse for the feudal lord was established, and an imperial bath ({{ill|Gomae-yu|ja|御前湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gomae-yu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was installed. === Modern and Contemporary Periods === In 1923 (Taishō 12), the Yamaga Onsen Railway (later known as the {{ill|Yamaga Onsen Railway|ja|山鹿温泉鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Onsen Railway|qq=}}) began operations, with {{ill|Yamaga Station|ja|山鹿駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Station|qq=}} serving as the nearest station; however, it was discontinued in 1960 (Shōwa 35) due to flooding. In November 2006 (Heisei 18), together with three other hot spring areas in the Kikuchi River basin—{{ill|Tama Onsen|ja|玉名温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tama Onsen|qq=}}, {{ill|Kikuchi Onsen|ja|菊池温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Onsen|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ueki Onsen|ja|植木温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueki Onsen|qq=}}—the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort Development Council" was established, aiming to brand the area as the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort" and to develop common products and wide-area tourism routes. == Access == * From the [[:en:Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal|Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal]], take the {{ill|Kyushu Sankō Bus|ja|九州産交バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu Sankō Bus|qq=}} bound for Yamaga Onsen and alight at "Yamaga Onsen (Yachiyo-za Entrance)." * Approximately 12 km from the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]] {{ill|Ueki Interchange|ja|植木インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueki Interchange|qq=}}, and 9.5 km from the {{ill|Kikusui Interchange|ja|菊水インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikusui Interchange|qq=}}. == Related Topics == * Hot springs in Yamaga City ** {{ill|Hirayama Onsen|ja|平山温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirayama Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Kumairi Onsen|ja|熊入温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumairi Onsen|qq=}} == External Links == issues|Yamaga Onsen Tourism Association|ja|山鹿温泉観光協会}]] * [https://yamaga-tanbou.jp/genre/onsen/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Yamaga Onsen|ja|山鹿温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Onsen|qq=}}] – Yamaga City {{Onsen}} {{Onsen-stub}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|山鹿温泉|version=104200222|comment=Imported full ja {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{混同|Yamakawa Onsen}} {{infobox Onsen | Name = Yamaga Onsen (山鹿温泉) | image = [[File:Sakura-yu Yamaga ac (1).jpg|280px]]
Communal bath {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura-yu|qq=}} | Comment = | Location = [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]][[:en:Yamaga, Kumamoto|Yamaga City]] {{Japanese Location Information|33|0|53.1|130|41|20.3|Yamaga Onsen|33.01475,130.688972|Yamaga Onsen|nocoord=yes}} {{Location map|Japan Kumamoto Prefecture|relief=1|float=center|width=225}} | Latitude Degrees = 33 | Latitude Minutes = 0 | Latitude Seconds = 53.1 | Longitude Degrees = 130 | Longitude Minutes = 41 | Longitude Seconds = 20.3 | Access = ''For details, see the [[#Access|Access]] section.'' | Spring Quality = {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} | Spring Temperature = | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = Alkaline | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = [https://yamaga-tanbou.jp/genre/onsen/ Official site of Yamaga Onsen in Yamaga City] | Special Notes = }} '''Yamaga Onsen''' (やまがおんせん) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]][[:en:Yamaga, Kumamoto|Yamaga City]] (formerly part of [[:en:Higo Province|Higo Province]]). It is renowned for the annual "Yamaga Lantern Festival" ({{ill|Yamaga Tōrō Matsuri|ja|山鹿灯籠まつり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Tōrō Matsuri|qq=}}) held every August. A communal bath (public bath) known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura-yu|qq=}} is available. == Spring Quality == * Alkaline simple hot spring It is reputed for its exceptionally smooth water that is said to have excellent beautifying effects. == Hot Spring Town == [[File:yamaga_ashiyu.jpg|thumb|"Ashiyu"]] There are approximately 20 establishments including [[:en:Ryokan|ryokan]] and [[:en:Hotel|hotels]], making it one of the largest hot spring towns in Kumamoto Prefecture. As an old hot spring town, it emphasizes its historic charm, and it is said that during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] it was modeled after [[:en:Dōgo Onsen|Dogo Onsen]]. Although it is located within the urban area, creatively designed open-air baths and bathing rooms enhance its quaint atmosphere. In addition to "Ashiyu" and communal baths, a hot spring pool (now no longer extant) was once established. Along the former {{ill|Buzen Highway|ja|豊前街道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buzen Highway|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, there stands the Yachiyo-za, a theater building completed in 1910 (Meiji 45). === Communal Bath === A communal bath known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura-yu|qq=}} exists. It was originally constructed by inviting the master carpenter who built the main building of {{ill|Dogo Onsen Honkan|ja|道後温泉本館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dogo Onsen Honkan|qq=}}. In a redevelopment in 1973 (Shōwa 48), it was incorporated into the Plaza Five Building, preserving only the original eave ([[:en:Bargeboard|hafu]]).{{Citation needed範囲|The interior was modern and at first resembled a pool, yet the water flowing from the lion-shaped spout retained the characteristic smoothness of Yamaga Onsen.|date=2024-08}} In July 2011 (Heisei 23), redevelopment work on the Plaza Five Building commenced, and in October 2012 (Heisei 24) a restoration of traditional Japanese architecture was completed.{{独自研究範囲|Primarily used by local residents.|date=2024-08}}{{Citation needed範囲|A drinking facility existed in front of the old building’s entrance, providing potable hot spring water 24 hours a day.|date=2024-08}} == History == === Origins === In ancient times, the {{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|lt=Wamyo Ruijusho|ja|倭名類聚抄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wamyo Ruijusho|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=和名類聚抄}}, a dictionary from the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], records the place name "Yun" (温泉) in "Yamaga-gun" (山鹿郡). Yamaga Onsen is considered one of the oldest hot spring resorts in Kumamoto Prefecture, and several legends regarding its origin persist. One theory posits that during the [[:en:Hōgen rebellion|Hōgen Rebellion]], after being defeated and retreating from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Uno Chika-ji|ja|宇野親治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uno Chika-ji|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=源親治}} was hunting in the mountains and discovered the hot spring upon seeing a herd of {{ill|deer|ja|鹿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/deer|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=シカ}} lying in a valley. The name "Yamaga" is said to derive from this {{ill|Hot Spring Discovery Legend|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Discovery Legend|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}. Another tradition holds that a descendant of Kikuchi Noritaka’s second son, Masataka—namely, the progenitor of Yamaga Sogyo, {{ill|Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|ja|山鹿太郎重光|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—relocated people to the area in the 3rd year of the [[:en:Jishō|Jishō period]] and constructed bathing facilities, thus laying the foundation for the growth of Yamaga town. === Early Modern Period === During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], as a post town, Yamaga Onsen flourished to the extent that it was said there was "not a single one of a thousand houses missing." At the site where Uno Chika-ji discovered the deer, a bathhouse for the feudal lord was established, and an imperial bath ({{ill|Gomae-yu|ja|御前湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gomae-yu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was installed. === Modern and Contemporary Periods === In 1923 (Taishō 12), the Yamaga Onsen Railway (later known as the {{ill|Yamaga Onsen Railway|ja|山鹿温泉鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Onsen Railway|qq=}}) began operations, with {{ill|Yamaga Station|ja|山鹿駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Station|qq=}} serving as the nearest station; however, it was discontinued in 1960 (Shōwa 35) due to flooding. In November 2006 (Heisei 18), together with three other hot spring areas in the Kikuchi River basin—{{ill|Tama Onsen|ja|玉名温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tama Onsen|qq=}}, {{ill|Kikuchi Onsen|ja|菊池温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Onsen|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ueki Onsen|ja|植木温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueki Onsen|qq=}}—the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort Development Council" was established, aiming to brand the area as the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort" and to develop common products and wide-area tourism routes. == Access == * From the [[:en:Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal|Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal]], take the {{ill|Kyushu Sankō Bus|ja|九州産交バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu Sankō Bus|qq=}} bound for Yamaga Onsen and alight at "Yamaga Onsen (Yachiyo-za Entrance)." * Approximately 12 km from the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]] {{ill|Ueki Interchange|ja|植木インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueki Interchange|qq=}}, and 9.5 km from the {{ill|Kikusui Interchange|ja|菊水インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikusui Interchange|qq=}}. == Related Topics == * Hot springs in Yamaga City ** {{ill|Hirayama Onsen|ja|平山温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirayama Onsen|qq=}} ** {{ill|Kumairi Onsen|ja|熊入温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumairi Onsen|qq=}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Yamaga Onsen}} {{Wiki Portal Link|Hot Springs|[[File:Hot springs 001.svg|34px|Portal: Hot Springs]]}} * [https://www.y-kankoukyoukai.com/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Yamaga Onsen Tourism Association|ja|山鹿温泉観光協会}]] * [https://yamaga-tanbou.jp/genre/onsen/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Yamaga Onsen|ja|山鹿温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Onsen|qq=}}] – Yamaga City {{Onsen}} {{Onsen-stub}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|山鹿温泉|version=104200222|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:E5%B1%B1%E9%B9%BF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89]] [[sn:Yamaga Onsen]] [[zh:%E5%B1%B1%E9%B9%BF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89]] [[Category:Yamaga, Kumamoto]] [[Category:Hot springs of Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Yamaga Onsen}} [[ja:山鹿温泉]] [[zh:山鹿温泉]] {{Commons category|Yamaga Onsen}} [[ja:山鹿温泉]] [[zh:山鹿温泉]] [[Category:paes with no wikidata]]
