Communal bath {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura-yu|1=Sakura-yu (Yamaga)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q129996964}} | Comment = | Location = [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]][[:en:Yamaga, Kumamoto|Yamaga City]] {{Japanese Location Information|33|0|53.1|130|41|20.3|Yamaga Onsen|33.01475,130.688972|Yamaga Onsen|nocoord=yes}} {{Location map|Japan Kumamoto Prefecture|relief=1|float=center|width=225}} | Latitude Degrees = 33 | Latitude Minutes = 0 | Latitude Seconds = 53.1 | Longitude Degrees = 130 | Longitude Minutes = 41 | Longitude Seconds = 20.3 | Access = ''For details, see the [[#Access|Access]] section.'' | Spring Quality = {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} | Spring Temperature = | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = Alkaline | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = [https://yamaga-tanbou.jp/genre/onsen/ Official site of Yamaga Onsen in Yamaga City] | Special Notes = }} '''Yamaga Onsen''' (やまがおんせん) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]][[:en:Yamaga, Kumamoto|Yamaga City]] (formerly part of [[:en:Higo Province|Higo Province]]). It is renowned for the annual "Yamaga Lantern Festival" ({{ill|Yamaga Tōrō Matsuri|ja|山鹿灯籠まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Tōrō Matsuri|1=Yamaga Lantern Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11471245}}) held every August. A communal bath (public bath) known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura-yu|1=Sakura-yu (Yamaga)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q129996964}} is available. == Spring Quality == * Alkaline simple hot spring It is reputed for its exceptionally smooth water that is said to have excellent beautifying effects. == Hot Spring Town == [[File:yamaga_ashiyu.jpg|thumb|"Ashiyu"]] There are approximately 20 establishments including [[:en:Ryokan|ryokan]] and [[:en:Hotel|hotels]], making it one of the largest hot spring towns in Kumamoto Prefecture. As an old hot spring town, it emphasizes its historic charm, and it is said that during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] it was modeled after [[:en:Dōgo Onsen|Dogo Onsen]]. Although it is located within the urban area, creatively designed open-air baths and bathing rooms enhance its quaint atmosphere. In addition to "Ashiyu" and communal baths, a hot spring pool (now no longer extant) was once established. Along the former {{ill|Buzen Highway|ja|豊前街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buzen Highway|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, there stands the Yachiyo-za, a theater building completed in 1910 (Meiji 45). === Communal Bath === A communal bath known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura-yu|1=Sakura-yu (Yamaga)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q129996964}} exists. It was originally constructed by inviting the master carpenter who built the main building of {{ill|Dogo Onsen Honkan|ja|道後温泉本館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dogo Onsen Honkan|1=Dōgo Onsen Honkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641713}}. In a redevelopment in 1973 (Shōwa 48), it was incorporated into the Plaza Five Building, preserving only the original eave ([[:en:Bargeboard|hafu]]).{{Citation needed範囲|The interior was modern and at first resembled a pool, yet the water flowing from the lion-shaped spout retained the characteristic smoothness of Yamaga Onsen.|date=2024-08}} In July 2011 (Heisei 23), redevelopment work on the Plaza Five Building commenced, and in October 2012 (Heisei 24) a restoration of traditional Japanese architecture was completed.{{独自研究範囲|Primarily used by local residents.|date=2024-08}}{{Citation needed範囲|A drinking facility existed in front of the old building’s entrance, providing potable hot spring water 24 hours a day.|date=2024-08}} == History == === Origins === In ancient times, the {{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|lt=Wamyo Ruijusho|ja|倭名類聚抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wamyo Ruijusho|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=和名類聚抄}}, a dictionary from the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], records the place name "Yun" (温泉) in "Yamaga-gun" (山鹿郡). Yamaga Onsen is considered one of the oldest hot spring resorts in Kumamoto Prefecture, and several legends regarding its origin persist. One theory posits that during the [[:en:Hōgen rebellion|Hōgen Rebellion]], after being defeated and retreating from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}}, {{ill|Uno Chika-ji|ja|宇野親治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uno Chika-ji|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=源親治|1=Uno Chika-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564304}} was hunting in the mountains and discovered the hot spring upon seeing a herd of {{ill|deer|ja|鹿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/deer|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=シカ|1=Cervidae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23390}} lying in a valley. The name "Yamaga" is said to derive from this {{ill|Hot Spring Discovery Legend|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Discovery Legend|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}. Another tradition holds that a descendant of Kikuchi Noritaka’s second son, Masataka—namely, the progenitor of Yamaga Sogyo, {{ill|Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|ja|山鹿太郎重光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—relocated people to the area in the 3rd year of the [[:en:Jishō|Jishō period]] and constructed bathing facilities, thus laying the foundation for the growth of Yamaga town. === Early Modern Period === During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], as a post town, Yamaga Onsen flourished to the extent that it was said there was "not a single one of a thousand houses missing." At the site where Uno Chika-ji discovered the deer, a bathhouse for the feudal lord was established, and an imperial bath ({{ill|Gomae-yu|ja|御前湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gomae-yu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was installed. === Modern and Contemporary Periods === In 1923 (Taishō 12), the Yamaga Onsen Railway (later known as the {{ill|Yamaga Onsen Railway|ja|山鹿温泉鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Onsen Railway|1=Yamaga Onsen Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11471246}}) began operations, with {{ill|Yamaga Station|ja|山鹿駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Station|1=Yamaga Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11471256}} serving as the nearest station; however, it was discontinued in 1960 (Shōwa 35) due to flooding. In November 2006 (Heisei 18), together with three other hot spring areas in the Kikuchi River basin—{{ill|Tama Onsen|ja|玉名温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tama Onsen|1=Tama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572449}}, {{ill|Kikuchi Onsen|ja|菊池温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Onsen|1=Kikuchi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619819}}, and {{ill|Ueki Onsen|ja|植木温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueki Onsen|1=Ueki Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540525}}—the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort Development Council" was established, aiming to brand the area as the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort" and to develop common products and wide-area tourism routes. == Access == * From the [[:en:Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal|Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal]], take the {{ill|Kyushu Sankō Bus|ja|九州産交バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu Sankō Bus|1=Kyushu Sanko Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370294}} bound for Yamaga Onsen and alight at "Yamaga Onsen (Yachiyo-za Entrance)." * Approximately 12 km from the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]] {{ill|Ueki Interchange|ja|植木インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueki Interchange|1=Ueki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1654414}}, and 9.5 km from the {{ill|Kikusui Interchange|ja|菊水インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikusui Interchange|1=Kikusui interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1655265}}. == Related Topics == * Hot springs in Yamaga City ** {{ill|Hirayama Onsen|ja|平山温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirayama Onsen|1=Hirayama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482537}} ** {{ill|Kumairi Onsen|ja|熊入温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumairi Onsen|1=Kumairi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11567841}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Yamaga Onsen}} {{Wiki Portal Link|Hot Springs|[[File:Hot springs 001.svg|34px|Portal: Hot Springs]]}} * [https://www.y-kankoukyoukai.com/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Yamaga Onsen Tourism Association|ja|山鹿温泉観光協会}]] * [https://yamaga-tanbou.jp/genre/onsen/ {{bad link|These It is renowned for the annual "Yamaga Lantern Festival" ({{ill|Yamaga Tōrō Matsuri|ja|山鹿灯籠まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Tōrō Matsuri|1=Yamaga Lantern Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11471245}}) held every August. A communal bath (public bath) known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura-yu|1=Sakura-yu (Yamaga)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q129996964}} is available. == Spring Quality == * Alkaline simple hot spring It is reputed for its exceptionally smooth water that is said to have excellent beautifying effects. == Hot Spring Town == [[File:yamaga_ashiyu.jpg|thumb|"Ashiyu"]] There are approximately 20 establishments including [[:en:Ryokan|ryokan]] and [[:en:Hotel|hotels]], making it one of the largest hot spring towns in Kumamoto Prefecture. As an old hot spring town, it emphasizes its historic charm, and it is said that during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] it was modeled after [[:en:Dōgo Onsen|Dogo Onsen]]. Although it is located within the urban area, creatively designed open-air baths and bathing rooms enhance its quaint atmosphere. In addition to "Ashiyu" and communal baths, a hot spring pool (now no longer extant) was once established. Along the former {{ill|Buzen Highway|ja|豊前街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buzen Highway|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, there stands the Yachiyo-za, a theater building completed in 1910 (Meiji 45). === Communal Bath === A communal bath known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura-yu|1=Sakura-yu (Yamaga)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q129996964}} exists. It was originally constructed by inviting the master carpenter who built the main building of {{ill|Dogo Onsen Honkan|ja|道後温泉本館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dogo Onsen Honkan|1=Dōgo Onsen Honkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641713}}. In a redevelopment in 1973 (Shōwa 48), it was incorporated into the Plaza Five Building, preserving only the original eave ([[:en:Bargeboard|hafu]]).{{Citation needed範囲|The interior was modern and at first resembled a pool, yet the water flowing from the lion-shaped spout retained the characteristic smoothness of Yamaga Onsen.|date=2024-08}} In July 2011 (Heisei 23), redevelopment work on the Plaza Five Building commenced, and in October 2012 (Heisei 24) a restoration of traditional Japanese architecture was completed.{{独自研究範囲|Primarily used by local residents.|date=2024-08}}{{Citation needed範囲|A drinking facility existed in front of the old building’s entrance, providing potable hot spring water 24 hours a day.|date=2024-08}} == History == === Origins === In ancient times, the {{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|lt=Wamyo Ruijusho|ja|倭名類聚抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wamyo Ruijusho|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=和名類聚抄}}, a dictionary from the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], records the place name "Yun" (温泉) in "Yamaga-gun" (山鹿郡). Yamaga Onsen is considered one of the oldest hot spring resorts in Kumamoto Prefecture, and several legends regarding its origin persist. One theory posits that during the [[:en:Hōgen rebellion|Hōgen Rebellion]], after being defeated and retreating from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}}, {{ill|Uno Chika-ji|ja|宇野親治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uno Chika-ji|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=源親治|1=Uno Chika-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564304}} was hunting in the mountains and discovered the hot spring upon seeing a herd of {{ill|deer|ja|鹿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/deer|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=シカ|1=Cervidae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23390}} lying in a valley. The name "Yamaga" is said to derive from this {{ill|Hot Spring Discovery Legend|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Discovery Legend|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}. Another tradition holds that a descendant of Kikuchi Noritaka’s second son, Masataka—namely, the progenitor of Yamaga Sogyo, {{ill|Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|ja|山鹿太郎重光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—relocated people to the area in the 3rd year of the [[:en:Jishō|Jishō period]] and constructed bathing facilities, thus laying the foundation for the growth of Yamaga town. === Early Modern Period === During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], as a post town, Yamaga Onsen flourished to the extent that it was said there was "not a single one of a thousand houses missing." At the site where Uno Chika-ji discovered the deer, a bathhouse for the feudal lord was established, and an imperial bath ({{ill|Gomae-yu|ja|御前湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gomae-yu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was installed. === Modern and Contemporary Periods === In 1923 (Taishō 12), the Yamaga Onsen Railway (later known as the {{ill|Yamaga Onsen Railway|ja|山鹿温泉鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Onsen Railway|1=Yamaga Onsen Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11471246}}) began operations, with {{ill|Yamaga Station|ja|山鹿駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Station|1=Yamaga Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11471256}} serving as the nearest station; however, it was discontinued in 1960 (Shōwa 35) due to flooding. In November 2006 (Heisei 18), together with three other hot spring areas in the Kikuchi River basin—{{ill|Tama Onsen|ja|玉名温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tama Onsen|1=Tama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572449}}, {{ill|Kikuchi Onsen|ja|菊池温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Onsen|1=Kikuchi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619819}}, and {{ill|Ueki Onsen|ja|植木温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueki Onsen|1=Ueki Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540525}}—the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort Development Council" was established, aiming to brand the area as the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort" and to develop common products and wide-area tourism routes. == Access == * From the [[:en:Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal|Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal]], take the {{ill|Kyushu Sankō Bus|ja|九州産交バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu Sankō Bus|1=Kyushu Sanko Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370294}} bound for Yamaga Onsen and alight at "Yamaga Onsen (Yachiyo-za Entrance)." * Approximately 12 km from the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]] {{ill|Ueki Interchange|ja|植木インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueki Interchange|1=Ueki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1654414}}, and 9.5 km from the {{ill|Kikusui Interchange|ja|菊水インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikusui Interchange|1=Kikusui interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1655265}}. == Related Topics == * Hot springs in Yamaga City ** {{ill|Hirayama Onsen|ja|平山温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirayama Onsen|1=Hirayama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482537}} ** {{ill|Kumairi Onsen|ja|熊入温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumairi Onsen|1=Kumairi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11567841}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Yamaga Onsen}} {{Wiki Portal Link|Hot Springs|[[File:Hot springs 001.svg|34px|Portal: Hot Springs]]}} * [https://www.y-kankoukyoukai.com/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Yamaga Onsen Tourism Association|ja|山鹿温泉観光協会}]] * [https://yamaga-tanbou.jp/genre/onsen/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Yamaga Onsen|ja|山鹿温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Onsen|qq=}}] – Yamaga City {{Onsen}} {{Onsen-stub}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|山鹿温泉|version=104200222|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[zh:%E5%B1%B1%E9%B9%BF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89]] {{Commons category|Yamaga Onsen}} [[ja:山鹿温泉]] [[zh:山鹿温泉]] {{Commons category|Yamaga Onsen}} [[ja:山鹿温泉]] [[zh:山鹿温泉]] {{wikidata link|Q11471244}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Hot springs of Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamaga, Kumamoto]]

Along the former {{ill|Buzen Highway|ja|豊前街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buzen Highway|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, there stands the Yachiyo-za, a theater building completed in 1910 (Meiji 45). === Communal Bath === A communal bath known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura-yu|1=Sakura-yu (Yamaga)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q129996964}} exists. It was originally constructed by inviting the master carpenter who built the main building of {{ill|Dogo Onsen Honkan|ja|道後温泉本館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dogo Onsen Honkan|1=Dōgo Onsen Honkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641713}}. In a redevelopment in 1973 (Shōwa 48), it was incorporated into the Plaza Five Building, preserving only the original eave ([[:en:Bargeboard|hafu]]).{{Citation needed範囲|The interior was modern and at first resembled a pool, yet the water flowing from the lion-shaped spout retained the characteristic smoothness of Yamaga Onsen.|date=2024-08}} In July 2011 (Heisei 23), redevelopment work on the Plaza Five Building commenced, and in October 2012 (Heisei 24) a restoration of traditional Japanese architecture was completed.{{独自研究範囲|Primarily used by local residents.|date=2024-08}}{{Citation needed範囲|A drinking facility existed in front of the old building’s entrance, providing potable hot spring water 24 hours a day.|date=2024-08}} == History == === Origins === In ancient times, the {{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|lt=Wamyo Ruijusho|ja|倭名類聚抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wamyo Ruijusho|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=和名類聚抄}}, a dictionary from the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], records the place name "Yun" (温泉) in "Yamaga-gun" (山鹿郡). Yamaga Onsen is considered one of the oldest hot spring resorts in Kumamoto Prefecture, and several legends regarding its origin persist. One theory posits that during the [[:en:Hōgen rebellion|Hōgen Rebellion]], after being defeated and retreating from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}}, {{ill|Uno Chika-ji|ja|宇野親治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uno Chika-ji|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=源親治|1=Uno Chika-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564304}} was hunting in the mountains and discovered the hot spring upon seeing a herd of {{ill|deer|ja|鹿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/deer|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=シカ|1=Cervidae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23390}} lying in a valley. The name "Yamaga" is said to derive from this {{ill|Hot Spring Discovery Legend|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Discovery Legend|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}. Another tradition holds that a descendant of Kikuchi Noritaka’s second son, Masataka—namely, the progenitor of Yamaga Sogyo, {{ill|Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|ja|山鹿太郎重光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—relocated people to the area in the 3rd year of the [[:en:Jishō|Jishō period]] and constructed bathing facilities, thus laying the foundation for the growth of Yamaga town. === Early Modern Period === During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], as a post town, Yamaga Onsen flourished to the extent that it was said there was "not a single one of a thousand houses missing." At the site where Uno Chika-ji discovered the deer, a bathhouse for the feudal lord was established, and an imperial bath ({{ill|Gomae-yu|ja|御前湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gomae-yu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was installed. === Modern and Contemporary Periods === In 1923 (Taishō 12), the Yamaga Onsen Railway (later known as the {{ill|Yamaga Onsen Railway|ja|山鹿温泉鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Onsen Railway|1=Yamaga Onsen Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11471246}}) began operations, with {{ill|Yamaga Station|ja|山鹿駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Station|1=Yamaga Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11471256}} serving as the nearest station; however, it was discontinued in 1960 (Shōwa 35) due to flooding. In November 2006 (Heisei 18), together with three other hot spring areas in the Kikuchi River basin—{{ill|Tama Onsen|ja|玉名温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tama Onsen|1=Tama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572449}}, {{ill|Kikuchi Onsen|ja|菊池温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Onsen|1=Kikuchi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619819}}, and {{ill|Ueki Onsen|ja|植木温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueki Onsen|1=Ueki Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540525}}—the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort Development Council" was established, aiming to brand the area as the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort" and to develop common products and wide-area tourism routes. == Access == * From the [[:en:Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal|Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal]], take the {{ill|Kyushu Sankō Bus|ja|九州産交バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu Sankō Bus|1=Kyushu Sanko Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370294}} bound for Yamaga Onsen and alight at "Yamaga Onsen (Yachiyo-za Entrance)." * Approximately 12 km from the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]] {{ill|Ueki Interchange|ja|植木インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueki Interchange|1=Ueki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1654414}}, and 9.5 km from the {{ill|Kikusui Interchange|ja|菊水インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikusui Interchange|1=Kikusui interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1655265}}. == Related Topics == * Hot springs in Yamaga City ** {{ill|Hirayama Onsen|ja|平山温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirayama Onsen|1=Hirayama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482537}} ** {{ill|Kumairi Onsen|ja|熊入温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumairi Onsen|1=Kumairi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11567841}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Yamaga Onsen}} {{Wiki Portal Link|Hot Springs|[[File:Hot springs 001.svg|34px|Portal: Hot Springs]]}} * [https://www.y-kankoukyoukai.com/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Yamaga Onsen Tourism Association|ja|山鹿温泉観光協会}]] * [https://yamaga-tanbou.jp/genre/onsen/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Yamaga Onsen|ja|山鹿温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Onsen|qq=}}] – Yamaga City {{Onsen}} {{Onsen-stub}} {{Onsen}} {{translated Onsen}} [[ja:山鹿温泉]] [[zh:山鹿温泉]] {{wikidata link|Q11471244}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Hot springs of Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English Communal bath {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura-yu|1=Sakura-yu (Yamaga)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q129996964}} | Comment = | Location = [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]][[:en:Yamaga, Kumamoto|Yamaga City]] {{Japanese Location Information|33|0|53.1|130|41|20.3|Yamaga Onsen|33.01475,130.688972|Yamaga Onsen|nocoord=yes}} {{Location map|Japan Kumamoto Prefecture|relief=1|float=center|width=225}} | Latitude Degrees = 33 | Latitude Minutes = 0 | Latitude Seconds = 53.1 | Longitude Degrees = 130 | Longitude Minutes = 41 | Longitude Seconds = 20.3 | Access = ''For details, see the [[#Access|Access]] section.'' | Spring Quality = {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} | Spring Temperature = | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = Alkaline | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = [https://yamaga-tanbou.jp/genre/onsen/ Official site of Yamaga Onsen in Yamaga City] | Special Notes = }} '''Yamaga Onsen''' (やまがおんせん) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]][[:en:Yamaga, Kumamoto|Yamaga City]] (formerly part of [[:en:Higo Province|Higo Province]]). It is renowned for the annual "Yamaga Lantern Festival" ({{ill|Yamaga Tōrō Matsuri|ja|山鹿灯籠まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Tōrō Matsuri|1=Yamaga Lantern Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11471245}}) held every August. A communal bath (public bath) known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura-yu|1=Sakura-yu (Yamaga)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q129996964}} is available. == Spring Quality == * Alkaline simple hot spring It is reputed for its exceptionally smooth water that is said to have excellent beautifying effects. == Hot Spring Town == [[File:yamaga_ashiyu.jpg|thumb|"Ashiyu"]] There are approximately 20 establishments including [[:en:Ryokan|ryokan]] and [[:en:Hotel|hotels]], making it one of the largest hot spring towns in Kumamoto Prefecture. As an old hot spring town, it emphasizes its historic charm, and it is said that during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] it was modeled after [[:en:Dōgo Onsen|Dogo Onsen]]. Although it is located within the urban area, creatively designed open-air baths and bathing rooms enhance its quaint atmosphere. In addition to "Ashiyu" and communal baths, a hot spring pool (now no longer extant) was once established. Along the former {{ill|Buzen Highway|ja|豊前街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buzen Highway|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, there stands the Yachiyo-za, a theater building completed in 1910 (Meiji 45). === Communal Bath === A communal bath known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura-yu|1=Sakura-yu (Yamaga)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q129996964}} exists. It was originally constructed by inviting the master carpenter who built the main building of {{ill|Dogo Onsen Honkan|ja|道後温泉本館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dogo Onsen Honkan|1=Dōgo Onsen Honkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641713}}. In a redevelopment in 1973 (Shōwa 48), it was incorporated into the Plaza Five Building, preserving only the original eave ([[:en:Bargeboard|hafu]]).{{Citation needed範囲|The interior was modern and at first resembled a pool, yet the water flowing from the lion-shaped spout retained the characteristic smoothness of Yamaga Onsen.|date=2024-08}} In July 2011 (Heisei 23), redevelopment work on the Plaza Five Building commenced, and in October 2012 (Heisei 24) a restoration of traditional Japanese architecture was completed.{{独自研究範囲|Primarily used by local residents.|date=2024-08}}{{Citation needed範囲|A drinking facility existed in front of the old building’s entrance, providing potable hot spring water 24 hours a day.|date=2024-08}} == History == === Origins === In ancient times, the {{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|lt=Wamyo Ruijusho|ja|倭名類聚抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wamyo Ruijusho|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=和名類聚抄}}, a dictionary from the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], records the place name "Yun" (温泉) in "Yamaga-gun" (山鹿郡). One theory posits that during the [[:en:Hōgen rebellion|Hōgen Rebellion]], after being defeated and retreating from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}}, {{ill|Uno Chika-ji|ja|宇野親治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uno Chika-ji|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=源親治|1=Uno Chika-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564304}} was hunting in the mountains and discovered the hot spring upon seeing a herd of {{ill|deer|ja|鹿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/deer|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=シカ|1=Cervidae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23390}} lying in a valley. The name "Yamaga" is said to derive from this {{ill|Hot Spring Discovery Legend|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Discovery Legend|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}. Another tradition holds that a descendant of Kikuchi Noritaka’s second son, Masataka—namely, the progenitor of Yamaga Sogyo, {{ill|Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|ja|山鹿太郎重光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—relocated people to the area in the 3rd year of the [[:en:Jishō|Jishō period]] and constructed bathing facilities, thus laying the foundation for the growth of Yamaga town. === Early Modern Period === During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], as a post town, Yamaga Onsen flourished to the extent that it was said there was "not a single one of a thousand houses missing." Along the former {{ill|Buzen Highway|ja|豊前街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buzen Highway|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, there stands the Yachiyo-za, a theater building completed in 1910 (Meiji 45). === Communal Bath === A communal bath known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura-yu|1=Sakura-yu (Yamaga)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q129996964}} exists. It was originally constructed by inviting the master carpenter who built the main building of {{ill|Dogo Onsen Honkan|ja|道後温泉本館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dogo Onsen Honkan|1=Dōgo Onsen Honkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641713}}. In a redevelopment in 1973 (Shōwa 48), it was incorporated into the Plaza Five Building, preserving only the original eave ({{ill|Bargeboard|en|Bargeboard|WD=Q4881511|lt=hafu}}).{{Citation needed範囲|The interior was modern and at first resembled a pool, yet the water flowing from the lion-shaped spout retained the characteristic smoothness of Yamaga Onsen.|date=2024-08}} In July 2011 (Heisei 23), redevelopment work on the Plaza Five Building commenced, and in October 2012 (Heisei 24) a restoration of traditional Japanese architecture was completed.{{独自研究範囲|Primarily used by local residents.|date=2024-08}}{{Citation needed範囲|A drinking facility existed in front of the old building’s entrance, providing potable hot spring water 24 hours a day.|date=2024-08}} == History == === Origins === In ancient times, the {{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|lt=Wamyo Ruijusho|ja|倭名類聚抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wamyo Ruijusho|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=和名類聚抄}}, a dictionary from the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, records the place name "Yun" (温泉) in "Yamaga-gun" (山鹿郡). Yamaga Onsen is considered one of the oldest hot spring resorts in Kumamoto Prefecture, and several legends regarding its origin persist. One theory posits that during the {{ill|Hōgen rebellion|en|Hōgen rebellion|WD=Q842357|lt=Hōgen Rebellion}}, after being defeated and retreating from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}}, {{ill|Uno Chika-ji|ja|宇野親治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uno Chika-ji|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=源親治|1=Uno Chika-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564304}} was hunting in the mountains and discovered the hot spring upon seeing a herd of {{ill|deer|ja|鹿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/deer|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=シカ|1=Cervidae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23390}} lying in a valley. The name "Yamaga" is said to derive from this {{ill|Hot Spring Discovery Legend|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Discovery Legend|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}. Another tradition holds that a descendant of Kikuchi Noritaka’s second son, Masataka—namely, the progenitor of Yamaga Sogyo, {{ill|Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|ja|山鹿太郎重光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—relocated people to the area in the 3rd year of the {{ill|Jishō|en|Jishō|WD=Q1194671|lt=Jishō period}} and constructed bathing facilities, thus laying the foundation for the growth of Yamaga town. === Early Modern Period === During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, as a post town, Yamaga Onsen flourished to the extent that it was said there was "not a single one of a thousand houses missing." At the site where Uno Chika-ji discovered the deer, a bathhouse for the feudal lord was established, and an imperial bath ({{ill|Gomae-yu|ja|御前湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gomae-yu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was installed. === Modern and Contemporary Periods === In 1923 (Taishō 12), the Yamaga Onsen Railway (later known as the {{ill|Yamaga Onsen Railway|ja|山鹿温泉鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Onsen Railway|1=Yamaga Onsen Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11471246}}) began operations, with {{ill|Yamaga Station|ja|山鹿駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Station|1=Yamaga Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11471256}} serving as the nearest station; however, it was discontinued in 1960 (Shōwa 35) due to flooding. In November 2006 (Heisei 18), together with three other hot spring areas in the Kikuchi River basin—{{ill|Tama Onsen|ja|玉名温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tama Onsen|1=Tama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572449}}, {{ill|Kikuchi Onsen|ja|菊池温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Onsen|1=Kikuchi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619819}}, and {{ill|Ueki Onsen|ja|植木温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueki Onsen|1=Ueki Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540525}}—the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort Development Council" was established, aiming to brand the area as the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort" and to develop common products and wide-area tourism routes. == Access == * From the {{ill|Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal|en|Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal|WD=Q6443418|lt=Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal}}, take the {{ill|Kyushu Sankō Bus|ja|九州産交バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu Sankō Bus|1=Kyushu Sanko Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370294}} bound for Yamaga Onsen and alight at "Yamaga Onsen (Yachiyo-za Entrance)." * Approximately 12 km from the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|en|Kyushu Expressway|WD=Q1060803|lt=Kyushu Expressway}} {{ill|Ueki Interchange|ja|植木インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueki Interchange|1=Ueki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1654414}}, and 9.5 km from the {{ill|Kikusui Interchange|ja|菊水インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikusui Interchange|1=Kikusui interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1655265}}. == Related Topics == * Hot springs in Yamaga City ** {{ill|Hirayama Onsen|ja|平山温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirayama Onsen|1=Hirayama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482537}} ** {{ill|Kumairi Onsen|ja|熊入温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumairi Onsen|1=Kumairi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11567841}} == External Links == issues|Yamaga Onsen Tourism Association|ja|山鹿温泉観光協会}]] * [https://yamaga-tanbou.jp/genre/onsen/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Yamaga Onsen|ja|山鹿温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Communal bath {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura-yu|1=Sakura-yu (Yamaga)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q129996964}} | Comment = | Location = {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|WD=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture}}{{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en|Yamaga, Kumamoto|WD=Q861214|lt=Yamaga City}} {{Japanese Location Information|33|0|53.1|130|41|20.3|Yamaga Onsen|33.01475,130.688972|Yamaga Onsen|nocoord=yes}} {{Location map|Japan Kumamoto Prefecture|relief=1|float=center|width=225}} | Latitude Degrees = 33 | Latitude Minutes = 0 | Latitude Seconds = 53.1 | Longitude Degrees = 130 | Longitude Minutes = 41 | Longitude Seconds = 20.3 | Access = ''For details, see the [[#Access|Access]] section.'' | Spring Quality = {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} | Spring Temperature = | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = Alkaline | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = [https://yamaga-tanbou.jp/genre/onsen/ Official site of Yamaga Onsen in Yamaga City] | Special Notes = }} '''Yamaga Onsen''' (やまがおんせん) is a {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen}} located in {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|WD=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture}}{{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en|Yamaga, Kumamoto|WD=Q861214|lt=Yamaga City}} (formerly part of {{ill|Higo Province|en|Higo Province|WD=Q259705|lt=Higo Province}}). It is renowned for the annual "Yamaga Lantern Festival" ({{ill|Yamaga Tōrō Matsuri|ja|山鹿灯籠まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Tōrō Matsuri|1=Yamaga Lantern Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11471245}}) held every August. A communal bath (public bath) known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura-yu|1=Sakura-yu (Yamaga)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q129996964}} is available. == Spring Quality == * Alkaline simple hot spring It is reputed for its exceptionally smooth water that is said to have excellent beautifying effects. == Hot Spring Town == [[File:yamaga_ashiyu.jpg|thumb|"Ashiyu"]] There are approximately 20 establishments including {{ill|Ryokan|en|Ryokan|WD=Q562930|lt=ryokan}} and {{ill|Hotel|en|Hotel|WD=Q27686|lt=hotels}}, making it one of the largest hot spring towns in Kumamoto Prefecture. As an old hot spring town, it emphasizes its historic charm, and it is said that during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}} it was modeled after {{ill|Dōgo Onsen|en|Dōgo Onsen|WD=Q1037748|lt=Dogo Onsen}}. Although it is located within the urban area, creatively designed open-air baths and bathing rooms enhance its quaint atmosphere. In addition to "Ashiyu" and communal baths, a hot spring pool (now no longer extant) was once established. Along the former {{ill|Buzen Highway|ja|豊前街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buzen Highway|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, there stands the Yachiyo-za, a theater building completed in 1910 (Meiji 45). === Communal Bath === A communal bath known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura-yu|1=Sakura-yu (Yamaga)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q129996964}} exists. It was originally constructed by inviting the master carpenter who built the main building of {{ill|Dogo Onsen Honkan|ja|道後温泉本館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dogo Onsen Honkan|1=Dōgo Onsen Honkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641713}}. In a redevelopment in 1973 (Shōwa 48), it was incorporated into the Plaza Five Building, preserving only the original eave ({{ill|Bargeboard|en|Bargeboard|WD=Q4881511|lt=hafu}}).{{Citation needed範囲|The interior was modern and at first resembled a pool, yet the water flowing from the lion-shaped spout retained the characteristic smoothness of Yamaga Onsen.|date=2024-08}} In July 2011 (Heisei 23), redevelopment work on the Plaza Five Building commenced, and in October 2012 (Heisei 24) a restoration of traditional Japanese architecture was completed.{{独自研究範囲|Primarily used by local residents.|date=2024-08}}{{Citation needed範囲|A drinking facility existed in front of the old building’s entrance, providing potable hot spring water 24 hours a day.|date=2024-08}} == History == === Origins === In ancient times, the {{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|lt=Wamyo Ruijusho|ja|倭名類聚抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wamyo Ruijusho|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=和名類聚抄}}, a dictionary from the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, records the place name "Yun" (温泉) in "Yamaga-gun" (山鹿郡). Yamaga Onsen is considered one of the oldest hot spring resorts in Kumamoto Prefecture, and several legends regarding its origin persist. One theory posits that during the {{ill|Hōgen rebellion|en|Hōgen rebellion|WD=Q842357|lt=Hōgen Rebellion}}, after being defeated and retreating from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}}, {{ill|Uno Chika-ji|ja|宇野親治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uno Chika-ji|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=源親治|1=Uno Chika-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564304}} was hunting in the mountains and discovered the hot spring upon seeing a herd of {{ill|deer|ja|鹿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/deer|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=シカ|1=Cervidae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23390}} lying in a valley. The name "Yamaga" is said to derive from this {{ill|Hot Spring Discovery Legend|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Discovery Legend|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}. Another tradition holds that a descendant of Kikuchi Noritaka’s second son, Masataka—namely, the progenitor of Yamaga Sogyo, {{ill|Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|ja|山鹿太郎重光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—relocated people to the area in the 3rd year of the {{ill|Jishō|en|Jishō|WD=Q1194671|lt=Jishō period}} and constructed bathing facilities, thus laying the foundation for the growth of Yamaga town. === Early Modern Period === During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, as a post town, Yamaga Onsen flourished to the extent that it was said there was "not a single one of a thousand houses missing." At the site where Uno Chika-ji discovered the deer, a bathhouse for the feudal lord was established, and an imperial bath ({{ill|Gomae-yu|ja|御前湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gomae-yu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was installed. === Modern and Contemporary Periods === In 1923 (Taishō 12), the Yamaga Onsen Railway (later known as the {{ill|Yamaga Onsen Railway|ja|山鹿温泉鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Onsen Railway|1=Yamaga Onsen Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11471246}}) began operations, with {{ill|Yamaga Station|ja|山鹿駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Station|1=Yamaga Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11471256}} serving as the nearest station; however, it was discontinued in 1960 (Shōwa 35) due to flooding. In November 2006 (Heisei 18), together with three other hot spring areas in the Kikuchi River basin—{{ill|Tama Onsen|ja|玉名温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tama Onsen|1=Tama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572449}}, {{ill|Kikuchi Onsen|ja|菊池温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Onsen|1=Kikuchi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619819}}, and {{ill|Ueki Onsen|ja|植木温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueki Onsen|1=Ueki Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540525}}—the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort Development Council" was established, aiming to brand the area as the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort" and to develop common products and wide-area tourism routes. == Access == * From the {{ill|Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal|en|Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal|WD=Q6443418|lt=Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal}}, take the {{ill|Kyushu Sankō Bus|ja|九州産交バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu Sankō Bus|1=Kyushu Sanko Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370294}} bound for Yamaga Onsen and alight at "Yamaga Onsen (Yachiyo-za Entrance)." * Approximately 12 km from the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|en|Kyushu Expressway|WD=Q1060803|lt=Kyushu Expressway}} {{ill|Ueki Interchange|ja|植木インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueki Interchange|1=Ueki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1654414}}, and 9.5 km from the {{ill|Kikusui Interchange|ja|菊水インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikusui Interchange|1=Kikusui interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1655265}}. == Related Topics == * Hot springs in Yamaga City ** {{ill|Hirayama Onsen|ja|平山温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirayama Onsen|1=Hirayama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482537}} ** {{ill|Kumairi Onsen|ja|熊入温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumairi Onsen|1=Kumairi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11567841}} == External Links == In addition to "Ashiyu" and communal baths, a hot spring pool (now no longer extant) was once established. Along the former {{ill|Buzen Highway|ja|豊前街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buzen Highway|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, there stands the Yachiyo-za, a theater building completed in 1910 (Meiji 45). === Communal Bath === A communal bath known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura-yu|1=Sakura-yu (Yamaga)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q129996964}} exists. It was originally constructed by inviting the master carpenter who built the main building of {{ill|Dogo Onsen Honkan|ja|道後温泉本館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dogo Onsen Honkan|1=Dōgo Onsen Honkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641713}}. In a redevelopment in 1973 (Shōwa 48), it was incorporated into the Plaza Five Building, preserving only the original eave ({{ill|Bargeboard|en|Bargeboard|WD=Q4881511|lt=hafu}}).{{Citation needed範囲|The interior was modern and at first resembled a pool, yet the water flowing from the lion-shaped spout retained the characteristic smoothness of Yamaga Onsen.|date=2024-08}} In July 2011 (Heisei 23), redevelopment work on the Plaza Five Building commenced, and in October 2012 (Heisei 24) a restoration of traditional Japanese architecture was completed.{{独自研究範囲|Primarily used by local residents.|date=2024-08}}{{Citation needed範囲|A drinking facility existed in front of the old building’s entrance, providing potable hot spring water 24 hours a day.|date=2024-08}} == History == === Origins === In ancient times, the {{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|lt=Wamyo Ruijusho|ja|倭名類聚抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wamyo Ruijusho|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=和名類聚抄}}, a dictionary from the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, records the place name "Yun" (温泉) in "Yamaga-gun" (山鹿郡). Yamaga Onsen is considered one of the oldest hot spring resorts in Kumamoto Prefecture, and several legends regarding its origin persist. One theory posits that during the {{ill|Hōgen rebellion|en|Hōgen rebellion|WD=Q842357|lt=Hōgen Rebellion}}, after being defeated and retreating from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}}, {{ill|Uno Chika-ji|ja|宇野親治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uno Chika-ji|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=源親治|1=Uno Chika-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564304}} was hunting in the mountains and discovered the hot spring upon seeing a herd of {{ill|deer|ja|鹿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/deer|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=シカ|1=Cervidae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23390}} lying in a valley. The name "Yamaga" is said to derive from this {{ill|Hot Spring Discovery Legend|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Discovery Legend|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}. Another tradition holds that a descendant of Kikuchi Noritaka’s second son, Masataka—namely, the progenitor of Yamaga Sogyo, {{ill|Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|ja|山鹿太郎重光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—relocated people to the area in the 3rd year of the {{ill|Jishō|en|Jishō|WD=Q1194671|lt=Jishō period}} and constructed bathing facilities, thus laying the foundation for the growth of Yamaga town. === Early Modern Period === During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, as a post town, Yamaga Onsen flourished to the extent that it was said there was "not a single one of a thousand houses missing." At the site where Uno Chika-ji discovered the deer, a bathhouse for the feudal lord was established, and an imperial bath ({{ill|Gomae-yu|ja|御前湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gomae-yu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was installed. === Modern and Contemporary Periods === In 1923 (Taishō 12), the Yamaga Onsen Railway (later known as the {{ill|Yamaga Onsen Railway|ja|山鹿温泉鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Onsen Railway|1=Yamaga Onsen Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11471246}}) began operations, with {{ill|Yamaga Station|ja|山鹿駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Station|1=Yamaga Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11471256}} serving as the nearest station; however, it was discontinued in 1960 (Shōwa 35) due to flooding. In November 2006 (Heisei 18), together with three other hot spring areas in the Kikuchi River basin—{{ill|Tama Onsen|ja|玉名温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tama Onsen|1=Tama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572449}}, {{ill|Kikuchi Onsen|ja|菊池温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Onsen|1=Kikuchi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619819}}, and {{ill|Ueki Onsen|ja|植木温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueki Onsen|1=Ueki Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540525}}—the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort Development Council" was established, aiming to brand the area as the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort" and to develop common products and wide-area tourism routes. == Access == * From the {{ill|Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal|en|Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal|WD=Q6443418|lt=Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal}}, take the {{ill|Kyushu Sankō Bus|ja|九州産交バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu Sankō Bus|1=Kyushu Sanko Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370294}} bound for Yamaga Onsen and alight at "Yamaga Onsen (Yachiyo-za Entrance)." * Approximately 12 km from the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|en|Kyushu Expressway|WD=Q1060803|lt=Kyushu Expressway}} {{ill|Ueki Interchange|ja|植木インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueki Interchange|1=Ueki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1654414}}, and 9.5 km from the {{ill|Kikusui Interchange|ja|菊水インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikusui Interchange|1=Kikusui interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1655265}}. == Related Topics == * Hot springs in Yamaga City ** {{ill|Hirayama Onsen|ja|平山温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirayama Onsen|1=Hirayama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482537}} ** {{ill|Kumairi Onsen|ja|熊入温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumairi Onsen|1=Kumairi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11567841}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Yamaga Onsen}} {{Wiki Portal Link|Hot Springs|[[File:Hot springs 001.svg|34px|Portal: Hot Springs]]}} * [https://www.y-kankoukyoukai.com/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Yamaga Onsen Tourism Association|ja|山鹿温泉観光協会}]] * [https://yamaga-tanbou.jp/genre/onsen/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Yamaga Onsen|ja|山鹿温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Onsen|qq=}}] – Yamaga City {{Onsen}} {{Onsen-stub}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|山鹿温泉|version=104200222|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[zh:%E5%B1%B1%E9%B9%BF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89]] {{Commons Communal bath {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura-yu|1=Sakura-yu (Yamaga)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q129996964}} | Comment = | Location = {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|WD=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture}}{{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en|Yamaga, Kumamoto|WD=Q861214|lt=Yamaga City}} {{Japanese Location Information|33|0|53.1|130|41|20.3|Yamaga Onsen|33.01475,130.688972|Yamaga Onsen|nocoord=yes}} {{Location map|Japan Kumamoto Prefecture|relief=1|float=center|width=225}} | Latitude Degrees = 33 | Latitude Minutes = 0 | Latitude Seconds = 53.1 | Longitude Degrees = 130 | Longitude Minutes = 41 | Longitude Seconds = 20.3 | Access = ''For details, see the [[#Access|Access]] section.'' | Spring Quality = {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} | Spring Temperature = | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = Alkaline | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = [https://yamaga-tanbou.jp/genre/onsen/ Official site of Yamaga Onsen in Yamaga City] | Special Notes = }} '''Yamaga Onsen''' (やまがおんせん) is a {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen}} located in {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|WD=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture}}{{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en|Yamaga, Kumamoto|WD=Q861214|lt=Yamaga City}} (formerly part of {{ill|Higo Province|en|Higo Province|WD=Q259705|lt=Higo Province}}). It is renowned for the annual "Yamaga Lantern Festival" ({{ill|Yamaga Tōrō Matsuri|ja|山鹿灯籠まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Tōrō Matsuri|1=Yamaga Lantern Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11471245}}) held every August. A communal bath (public bath) known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura-yu|1=Sakura-yu (Yamaga)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q129996964}} is available. == Spring Quality == * Alkaline simple hot spring It is reputed for its exceptionally smooth water that is said to have excellent beautifying effects. == Hot Spring Town == [[File:yamaga_ashiyu.jpg|thumb|"Ashiyu"]] There are approximately 20 establishments including {{ill|Ryokan|en|Ryokan|WD=Q562930|lt=ryokan}} and {{ill|Hotel|en|Hotel|WD=Q27686|lt=hotels}}, making it one of the largest hot spring towns in Kumamoto Prefecture. As an old hot spring town, it emphasizes its historic charm, and it is said that during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}} it was modeled after {{ill|Dōgo Onsen|en|Dōgo Onsen|WD=Q1037748|lt=Dogo Onsen}}. Although it is located within the urban area, creatively designed open-air baths and bathing rooms enhance its quaint atmosphere. In addition to "Ashiyu" and communal baths, a hot spring pool (now no longer extant) was once established. Along the former {{ill|Buzen Highway|ja|豊前街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buzen Highway|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, there stands the Yachiyo-za, a theater building completed in 1910 (Meiji 45). === Communal Bath === A communal bath known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura-yu|1=Sakura-yu (Yamaga)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q129996964}} exists. It was originally constructed by inviting the master carpenter who built the main building of {{ill|Dogo Onsen Honkan|ja|道後温泉本館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dogo Onsen Honkan|1=Dōgo Onsen Honkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641713}}. In a redevelopment in 1973 (Shōwa 48), it was incorporated into the Plaza Five Building, preserving only the original eave ({{ill|Bargeboard|en|Bargeboard|WD=Q4881511|lt=hafu}}).{{Citation needed範囲|The interior was modern and at first resembled a pool, yet the water flowing from the lion-shaped spout retained the characteristic smoothness of Yamaga Onsen.|date=2024-08}} In July 2011 (Heisei 23), redevelopment work on the Plaza Five Building commenced, and in October 2012 (Heisei 24) a restoration of traditional Japanese architecture was completed.{{独自研究範囲|Primarily used by local residents.|date=2024-08}}{{Citation needed範囲|A drinking facility existed in front of the old building’s entrance, providing potable hot spring water 24 hours a day.|date=2024-08}} == History == === Origins === In ancient times, the {{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|lt=Wamyo Ruijusho|ja|倭名類聚抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wamyo Ruijusho|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=和名類聚抄}}, a dictionary from the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, records the place name "Yun" (温泉) in "Yamaga-gun" (山鹿郡). Yamaga Onsen is considered one of the oldest hot spring resorts in Kumamoto Prefecture, and several legends regarding its origin persist. One theory posits that during the {{ill|Hōgen rebellion|en|Hōgen rebellion|WD=Q842357|lt=Hōgen Rebellion}}, after being defeated and retreating from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}}, {{ill|Uno Chika-ji|ja|宇野親治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uno Chika-ji|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=源親治|1=Uno Chika-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564304}} was hunting in the mountains and discovered the hot spring upon seeing a herd of {{ill|deer|ja|鹿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/deer|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=シカ|1=Cervidae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23390}} lying in a valley. The name "Yamaga" is said to derive from this {{ill|Hot Spring Discovery Legend|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Discovery Legend|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}. Another tradition holds that a descendant of Kikuchi Noritaka’s second son, Masataka—namely, the progenitor of Yamaga Sogyo, {{ill|Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|ja|山鹿太郎重光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—relocated people to the area in the 3rd year of the {{ill|Jishō|en|Jishō|WD=Q1194671|lt=Jishō period}} and constructed bathing facilities, thus laying the foundation for the growth of Yamaga town. === Early Modern Period === During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, as a post town, Yamaga Onsen flourished to the extent that it was said there was "not a single one of a thousand houses missing." At the site where Uno Chika-ji discovered the deer, a bathhouse for the feudal lord was established, and an imperial bath ({{ill|Gomae-yu|ja|御前湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gomae-yu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was installed. === Modern and Contemporary Periods === In 1923 (Taishō 12), the Yamaga Onsen Railway (later known as the {{ill|Yamaga Onsen Railway|ja|山鹿温泉鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Onsen Railway|1=Yamaga Onsen Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11471246}}) began operations, with {{ill|Yamaga Station|ja|山鹿駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Station|1=Yamaga Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11471256}} serving as the nearest station; however, it was discontinued in 1960 (Shōwa 35) due to flooding. based on wikidata|WD=Q11572449}}, {{ill|Kikuchi Onsen|ja|菊池温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Onsen|1=Kikuchi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619819}}, and {{ill|Ueki Onsen|ja|植木温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueki Onsen|1=Ueki Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540525}}—the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort Development Council" was established, aiming to brand the area as the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort" and to develop common products and wide-area tourism routes. == Access == * From the {{ill|Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal|en|Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal|WD=Q6443418|lt=Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal}}, take the {{ill|Kyushu Sankō Bus|ja|九州産交バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu Sankō Bus|1=Kyushu Sanko Bus|comment=changed Along the former {{ill|Buzen Highway|ja|豊前街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buzen Highway|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, there stands the Yachiyo-za, a theater building completed in 1910 (Meiji 45). === Communal Bath === A communal bath known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura-yu|1=Sakura-yu (Yamaga)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q129996964}} exists. It was originally constructed by inviting the master carpenter who built the main building of {{ill|Dogo Onsen Honkan|ja|道後温泉本館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dogo Onsen Honkan|1=Dōgo Onsen Honkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641713}}. In a redevelopment in 1973 (Shōwa 48), it was incorporated into the Plaza Five Building, preserving only the original eave ({{ill|Bargeboard|en|Bargeboard|WD=Q4881511|lt=hafu}}).{{Citation needed範囲|The interior was modern and at first resembled a pool, yet the water flowing from the lion-shaped spout retained the characteristic smoothness of Yamaga Onsen.|date=2024-08}} In July 2011 (Heisei 23), redevelopment work on the Plaza Five Building commenced, and in October 2012 (Heisei 24) a restoration of traditional Japanese architecture was completed.{{独自研究範囲|Primarily used by local residents.|date=2024-08}}{{Citation needed範囲|A drinking facility existed in front of the old building’s entrance, providing potable hot spring water 24 hours a day.|date=2024-08}} == History == === Origins === In ancient times, the {{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|lt=Wamyo Ruijusho|ja|倭名類聚抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wamyo Ruijusho|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=和名類聚抄}}, a dictionary from the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, records the place name "Yun" (温泉) in "Yamaga-gun" (山鹿郡). Yamaga Onsen is considered one of the oldest hot spring resorts in Kumamoto Prefecture, and several legends regarding its origin persist. One theory posits that during the {{ill|Hōgen rebellion|en|Hōgen rebellion|WD=Q842357|lt=Hōgen Rebellion}}, after being defeated and retreating from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}}, {{ill|Uno Chika-ji|ja|宇野親治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uno Chika-ji|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=源親治|1=Uno Chika-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564304}} was hunting in the mountains and discovered the hot spring upon seeing a herd of {{ill|deer|ja|鹿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/deer|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=シカ|1=Cervidae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23390}} lying in a valley. The name "Yamaga" is said to derive from this {{ill|Hot Spring Discovery Legend|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Discovery Legend|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}. Another tradition holds that a descendant of Kikuchi Noritaka’s second son, Masataka—namely, the progenitor of Yamaga Sogyo, {{ill|Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|ja|山鹿太郎重光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—relocated people to the area in the 3rd year of the {{ill|Jishō|en|Jishō|WD=Q1194671|lt=Jishō period}} and constructed bathing facilities, thus laying the foundation for the growth of Yamaga town. === Early Modern Period === During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, as a post town, Yamaga Onsen flourished to the extent that it was said there was "not a single one of a thousand houses missing." At the site where Uno Chika-ji discovered the deer, a bathhouse for the feudal lord was established, and an imperial bath ({{ill|Gomae-yu|ja|御前湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gomae-yu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was installed. === Modern and Contemporary Periods === In 1923 (Taishō 12), the Yamaga Onsen Railway (later known as the {{ill|Yamaga Onsen Railway|ja|山鹿温泉鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Onsen Railway|1=Yamaga Onsen Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11471246}}) began operations, with {{ill|Yamaga Station|ja|山鹿駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Station|1=Yamaga Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11471256}} serving as the nearest station; however, it was discontinued in 1960 (Shōwa 35) due to flooding. In November 2006 (Heisei 18), together with three other hot spring areas in the Kikuchi River basin—{{ill|Tama Onsen|ja|玉名温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tama Onsen|1=Tama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572449}}, {{ill|Kikuchi Onsen|ja|菊池温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Onsen|1=Kikuchi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619819}}, and {{ill|Ueki Onsen|ja|植木温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueki Onsen|1=Ueki Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540525}}—the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort Development Council" was established, aiming to brand the area as the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort" and to develop common products and wide-area tourism routes. == Access == * From the {{ill|Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal|en|Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal|WD=Q6443418|lt=Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal}}, take the {{ill|Kyushu Sankō Bus|ja|九州産交バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu Sankō Bus|1=Kyushu Sanko Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370294}} bound for Yamaga Onsen and alight at "Yamaga Onsen (Yachiyo-za Entrance)." * Approximately 12 km from the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|en|Kyushu Expressway|WD=Q1060803|lt=Kyushu Expressway}} {{ill|Ueki Interchange|ja|植木インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueki Interchange|1=Ueki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1654414}}, and 9.5 km from the {{ill|Kikusui Interchange|ja|菊水インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikusui Interchange|1=Kikusui interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1655265}}. == Related Topics == * Hot springs in Yamaga City ** {{ill|Hirayama Onsen|ja|平山温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirayama Onsen|1=Hirayama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482537}} ** {{ill|Kumairi Onsen|ja|熊入温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumairi Onsen|1=Kumairi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11567841}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Yamaga Onsen}} {{Wiki Portal Link|Hot Springs|[[File:Hot springs 001.svg|34px|Portal: Hot Springs]]}} * [https://www.y-kankoukyoukai.com/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Yamaga Onsen Tourism Association|ja|山鹿温泉観光協会}]] * [https://yamaga-tanbou.jp/genre/onsen/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Yamaga Onsen|ja|山鹿温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Onsen|qq=}}] – Yamaga City {{Onsen}} {{Onsen-stub}} {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|山鹿温泉|version=104200222|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[zh:%E5%B1%B1%E9%B9%BF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89]] {{Commons category|Yamaga Onsen}} [[ja:山鹿温泉]] [[zh:山鹿温泉]] {{Commons category|Yamaga Onsen}} [[ja:山鹿温泉]] [[zh:山鹿温泉]] {{wikidata link|Q11471244}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Hot springs of Kumamoto Communal bath {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura-yu|1=Sakura-yu (Yamaga)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q129996964}} | Comment = | Location = {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|WD=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture}}{{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en|Yamaga, Kumamoto|WD=Q861214|lt=Yamaga City}} {{Japanese Location Information|33|0|53.1|130|41|20.3|Yamaga Onsen|33.01475,130.688972|Yamaga Onsen|nocoord=yes}} {{Location map|Japan Kumamoto Prefecture|relief=1|float=center|width=225}} | Latitude Degrees = 33 | Latitude Minutes = 0 | Latitude Seconds = 53.1 | Longitude Degrees = 130 | Longitude Minutes = 41 | Longitude Seconds = 20.3 | Access = ''For details, see the [[#Access|Access]] section.'' | Spring Quality = {{ill|Simple Hot Spring|ja|単純温泉|1=Simple Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Simple Hot Springs|qq=}} | Spring Temperature = | Flow Rate = | pH = | Classification of Liquid Nature = Alkaline | Classification of Osmotic Pressure = | Number of Lodging Facilities = | Total Capacity = | Annual Number of Bathers = | Statistical Year = | External Links = [https://yamaga-tanbou.jp/genre/onsen/ Official site of Yamaga Onsen in Yamaga City] | Special Notes = }} '''Yamaga Onsen''' (やまがおんせん) is a {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen}} located in {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|WD=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture}}{{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en|Yamaga, Kumamoto|WD=Q861214|lt=Yamaga City}} (formerly part of {{ill|Higo Province|en|Higo Province|WD=Q259705|lt=Higo Province}}). It is renowned for the annual "Yamaga Lantern Festival" ({{ill|Yamaga Tōrō Matsuri|ja|山鹿灯籠まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Tōrō Matsuri|1=Yamaga Lantern Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11471245}}) held every August. A communal bath (public bath) known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura-yu|1=Sakura-yu (Yamaga)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q129996964}} is available. == Spring Quality == * Alkaline simple hot spring It is reputed for its exceptionally smooth water that is said to have excellent beautifying effects. == Hot Spring Town == [[File:yamaga_ashiyu.jpg|thumb|"Ashiyu"]] There are approximately 20 establishments including {{ill|Ryokan|en|Ryokan|WD=Q562930|lt=ryokan}} and {{ill|Hotel|en|Hotel|WD=Q27686|lt=hotels}}, making it one of the largest hot spring towns in Kumamoto Prefecture. As an old hot spring town, it emphasizes its historic charm, and it is said that during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}} it was modeled after {{ill|Dōgo Onsen|en|Dōgo Onsen|WD=Q1037748|lt=Dogo Onsen}}. Although it is located within the urban area, creatively designed open-air baths and bathing rooms enhance its quaint atmosphere. In addition to "Ashiyu" and communal baths, a hot spring pool (now no longer extant) was once established. Along the former {{ill|Buzen Highway|ja|豊前街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buzen Highway|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, there stands the Yachiyo-za, a theater building completed in 1910 (Meiji 45). === Communal Bath === A communal bath known as {{ill|Sakura-yu|ja|さくら湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakura-yu|1=Sakura-yu (Yamaga)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q129996964}} exists. It was originally constructed by inviting the master carpenter who built the main building of {{ill|Dogo Onsen Honkan|ja|道後温泉本館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dogo Onsen Honkan|1=Dōgo Onsen Honkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641713}}. In a redevelopment in 1973 (Shōwa 48), it was incorporated into the Plaza Five Building, preserving only the original eave ({{ill|Bargeboard|en|Bargeboard|WD=Q4881511|lt=hafu}}).{{Citation needed範囲|The interior was modern and at first resembled a pool, yet the water flowing from the lion-shaped spout retained the characteristic smoothness of Yamaga Onsen.|date=2024-08}} In July 2011 (Heisei 23), redevelopment work on the Plaza Five Building commenced, and in October 2012 (Heisei 24) a restoration of traditional Japanese architecture was completed.{{独自研究範囲|Primarily used by local residents.|date=2024-08}}{{Citation needed範囲|A drinking facility existed in front of the old building’s entrance, providing potable hot spring water 24 hours a day.|date=2024-08}} == History == === Origins === In ancient times, the {{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|lt=Wamyo Ruijusho|ja|倭名類聚抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wamyo Ruijusho|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=和名類聚抄}}, a dictionary from the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, records the place name "Yun" (温泉) in "Yamaga-gun" (山鹿郡). Yamaga Onsen is considered one of the oldest hot spring resorts in Kumamoto Prefecture, and several legends regarding its origin persist. One theory posits that during the {{ill|Hōgen rebellion|en|Hōgen rebellion|WD=Q842357|lt=Hōgen Rebellion}}, after being defeated and retreating from {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|1=Kyōto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q740246}}, {{ill|Uno Chika-ji|ja|宇野親治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uno Chika-ji|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=源親治|1=Uno Chika-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564304}} was hunting in the mountains and discovered the hot spring upon seeing a herd of {{ill|deer|ja|鹿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/deer|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=シカ|1=Cervidae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23390}} lying in a valley. The name "Yamaga" is said to derive from this {{ill|Hot Spring Discovery Legend|ja|開湯伝説|1=Onsen Origin Myth|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Discovery Legend|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Origin Myth|qq=}}. Another tradition holds that a descendant of Kikuchi Noritaka’s second son, Masataka—namely, the progenitor of Yamaga Sogyo, {{ill|Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|ja|山鹿太郎重光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Tarō Shigemitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—relocated people to the area in the 3rd year of the {{ill|Jishō|en|Jishō|WD=Q1194671|lt=Jishō period}} and constructed bathing facilities, thus laying the foundation for the growth of Yamaga town. === Early Modern Period === During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, as a post town, Yamaga Onsen flourished to the extent that it was said there was "not a single one of a thousand houses missing." At the site where Uno Chika-ji discovered the deer, a bathhouse for the feudal lord was established, and an imperial bath ({{ill|Gomae-yu|ja|御前湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gomae-yu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was installed. === Modern and Contemporary Periods === In 1923 (Taishō 12), the Yamaga Onsen Railway (later known as the {{ill|Yamaga Onsen Railway|ja|山鹿温泉鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Onsen Railway|1=Yamaga Onsen Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11471246}}) began operations, with {{ill|Yamaga Station|ja|山鹿駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga Station|1=Yamaga Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11471256}} serving as the nearest station; however, it was discontinued in 1960 (Shōwa 35) due to flooding. In November 2006 (Heisei 18), together with three other hot spring areas in the Kikuchi River basin—{{ill|Tama Onsen|ja|玉名温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tama Onsen|1=Tama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572449}}, {{ill|Kikuchi Onsen|ja|菊池温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Onsen|1=Kikuchi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619819}}, and {{ill|Ueki Onsen|ja|植木温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueki Onsen|1=Ueki Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540525}}—the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort Development Council" was established, aiming to brand the area as the "Kikuchi River Onsen Resort" and to develop common products and wide-area tourism routes. == Access == * From the {{ill|Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal|en|Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal|WD=Q6443418|lt=Kumamoto Sakuramachi Bus Terminal}}, take the {{ill|Kyushu Sankō Bus|ja|九州産交バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu Sankō Bus|1=Kyushu Sanko Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370294}} bound for Yamaga Onsen and alight at "Yamaga Onsen (Yachiyo-za Entrance)." * Approximately 12 km from the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|en|Kyushu Expressway|WD=Q1060803|lt=Kyushu Expressway}} {{ill|Ueki Interchange|ja|植木インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueki Interchange|1=Ueki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1654414}}, and 9.5 km from the {{ill|Kikusui Interchange|ja|菊水インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikusui Interchange|1=Kikusui interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1655265}}. == Related Topics == * Hot springs in Yamaga City ** {{ill|Hirayama Onsen|ja|平山温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirayama Onsen|1=Hirayama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482537}} ** {{ill|Kumairi Onsen|ja|熊入温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumairi Onsen|1=Kumairi Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11567841}} == External Links ==
